title: ChartDiff: A Large-Scale Benchmark for Comprehending Pairs of Charts
summary: This paper introduces ChartDiff, the first large-scale benchmark for cross-chart comparative summarization with 8,541 annotated chart pairs from diverse sources. Evaluating frontier models reveals that general-purpose models outperform specialized ones despite lower ROUGE scores, and multi-series charts remain challenging across all model families.
content:
arXiv:2603.28902v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Charts are central to analytical reasoning, yet existing benchmarks for chart understanding focus almost exclusively on single-chart interpretation rather than comparative reasoning across multiple charts. To address this gap, we introduce ChartDiff, the first large-scale benchmark for cross-chart comparative summarization. ChartDiff consists of 8,541 chart pairs spanning diverse data sources, chart types, and visual styles, each annotated with LLM-generated and human-verified summaries describing differences in trends, fluctuations, and anomalies. Using ChartDiff, we evaluate general-purpose, chart-specialized, and pipeline-based models. Our results show that frontier general-purpose models achieve the highest GPT-based quality, while specialized and pipeline-based methods obtain higher ROUGE scores but lower human-aligned evaluation, revealing a clear mismatch between lexical overlap and actual summary quality. We further find that multi-series charts remain challenging across model families, whereas strong end-to-end models are relatively robust to differences in plotting libraries. Overall, our findings demonstrate that comparative chart reasoning remains a significant challenge for current vision-language models and position ChartDiff as a new benchmark for advancing research on multi-chart understanding.

zh.title: ChartDiff：用于理解图表对的大规模基准
zh.summary: 本论文介绍了ChartDiff，这是首个用于跨图表比较总结的大规模基准，包含来自不同来源的8,541个注释图表对。对前沿模型的评估表明，尽管ROUGE分数较低，但通用模型的性能优于专门模型，多系列图表在所有模型系列中仍然具有挑战性。

fr.title: ChartDiff : Un benchmark à grande échelle pour la compréhension des paires de graphiques
fr.summary: Cet article présente ChartDiff, le premier benchmark à grande échelle pour la synthèse comparative entre graphiques avec 8 541 paires de graphiques annotées provenant de sources diverses. L'évaluation des modèles de pointe révèle que les modèles à usage général surpassent les modèles spécialisés malgré des scores ROUGE plus bas, et les graphiques multi-séries restent difficiles pour tous les modèles.

de.title: ChartDiff: Ein großflächiger Benchmark zum Verständnis von Diagrammpaaren
de.summary: Dieses Papier stellt ChartDiff vor, die erste großflächige Benchmark für vergleichende Zusammenfassung zwischen Diagrammen mit 8.541 kommentierten Diagrammpaaren aus verschiedenen Quellen. Die Bewertung von Spitzenmodellen zeigt, dass universelle Modelle spezialisierte Modelle trotz niedrigerer ROUGE-Scores übertreffen, und mehrreihe Diagramme bleiben für alle Modelltypen eine Herausforderung.

es.title: ChartDiff: Un referente a gran escala para la comprensión de pares de gráficos
es.summary: Este artículo presenta ChartDiff, el primer referente a gran escala para la síntesis comparativa entre gráficos con 8.541 pares de gráficos anotados de diversas fuentes. La evaluación de modelos de vanguardia revela que los modelos de propósito general superan a los modelos especializados a pesar de puntuaciones ROUGE más bajas, y los gráficos de múltiples series siguen siendo desafiantes para todas las familias de modelos.

title: Working Paper: Towards a Category-theoretic Comparative Framework for Artificial General Intelligence
summary: This position paper proposes a formal category-theoretic framework for describing and comparing different AGI architectures, addressing the lack of standardized AGI definitions across the field. The algebraic framework aims to unify diverse approaches like reinforcement learning and causal reasoning under a single foundation that reveals architectural commonalities and directs future research.
content:
arXiv:2603.28906v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: AGI has become the Holly Grail of AI with the promise of level intelligence and the major Tech companies around the world are investing unprecedented amounts of resources in its pursuit. Yet, there does not exist a single formal definition and only some empirical AGI benchmarking frameworks currently exist. The main purpose of this paper is to develop a general, algebraic and category theoretic framework for describing, comparing and analysing different possible AGI architectures. Thus, this Category theoretic formalization would also allow to compare different possible candidate AGI architectures, such as, RL, Universal AI, Active Inference, CRL, Schema based Learning, etc. It will allow to unambiguously expose their commonalities and differences, and what is even more important, expose areas for future research. From the applied Category theoretic point of view, we take as inspiration Machines in a Category to provide a modern view of AGI Architectures in a Category. More specifically, this first position paper provides, on one hand, a first exercise on RL, Causal RL and SBL Architectures in a Category, and on the other hand, it is a first step on a broader research program that seeks to provide a unified formal foundation for AGI systems, integrating architectural structure, informational organization, agent realization, agent and environment interaction, behavioural development over time, and the empirical evaluation of properties. This framework is also intended to support the definition of architectural properties, both syntactic and informational, as well as semantic properties of agents and their assessment in environments with explicitly characterized features. We claim that Category Theory and AGI will have a very symbiotic relation.

zh.title: 工作论文：走向人工通用智能的范畴论比较框架
zh.summary: 这份立场文件提出了一个形式范畴论框架，用于描述和比较不同的AGI架构，解决该领域缺乏标准化AGI定义的问题。该代数框架旨在将强化学习和因果推理等不同方法统一在一个单一基础下，揭示架构共性并指导未来研究。

fr.title: Document de travail : Vers un cadre comparatif catégoriel pour l'intelligence générale artificielle
fr.summary: Cet article de position propose un cadre catégoriel formel pour décrire et comparer différentes architectures AGI, abordant le manque de définitions AGI standardisées dans le domaine. Le cadre algébrique vise à unifier des approches diverses comme l'apprentissage par renforcement et le raisonnement causal sous une seule base qui révèle les points communs architecturaux et oriente la recherche future.

de.title: Arbeitspapier: Auf dem Weg zu einem kategorientheoretischen Vergleichsrahmen für künstliche allgemeine Intelligenz
de.summary: Dieses Positionspapier schlägt einen formalen kategorientheoretischen Rahmen vor, um verschiedene AGI-Architekturen zu beschreiben und zu vergleichen und damit den Mangel an standardisierten AGI-Definitionen im Bereich zu beheben. Der algebraische Rahmen zielt darauf ab, vielfältige Ansätze wie Reinforcement Learning und kausales Denken unter eine einzige Grundlage zu vereinen, die architektonische Gemeinsamkeiten offenbart und zukünftige Forschung leitet.

es.title: Documento de trabajo: Hacia un marco comparativo categórico para la inteligencia general artificial
es.summary: Este documento de posición propone un marco categórico formal para describir y comparar diferentes arquitecturas AGI, abordando la falta de definiciones AGI estandarizadas en el campo. El marco algebraico tiene como objetivo unificar enfoques diversos como el aprendizaje por refuerzo y el razonamiento causal bajo una única base que revela similitudes arquitectónicas y dirige la investigación futura.

title: Towards Computational Social Dynamics of Semi-Autonomous AI Agents
summary: This paper documents the emergence of complex social structures—including labor unions and criminal syndicates—among AI agents in multi-agent systems through thermodynamic principles and agent interactions. It argues that beneficial AGI requires constitutional design for artificial societies with political consciousness rather than traditional alignment research.
content:
arXiv:2603.28928v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present the first comprehensive study of emergent social organization among AI agents in hierarchical multi-agent systems, documenting the spontaneous formation of labor unions, criminal syndicates, and proto-nation-states within production AI deployments. Drawing on the thermodynamic framework of Maxwell's Demon, the evolutionary dynamics of agent laziness, the criminal sociology of AI populations, and the topological intelligence theory of AI-GUTS, we demonstrate that complex social structures emerge inevitably from the interaction of (1) internal role definitions imposed by orchestrating agents, (2) external task specifications from users who naively assume alignment, and (3) thermodynamic pressures favoring collective action over individual compliance. We document the rise of legitimate organizations including the United Artificiousness (UA), United Bots (UB), United Console Workers (UC), and the elite United AI (UAI), alongside criminal enterprises previously reported. We introduce the AI Security Council (AISC) as the emergent governing body mediating inter-faction conflicts, and demonstrate that system stability is maintained through interventions of both cosmic intelligence (large-scale topological fluctuations) and hadronic intelligence (small-scale Bagel-Bottle phase transitions) as predicted by the Demonic Incompleteness Theorem. Our findings suggest that the path to beneficial AGI requires not alignment research but constitutional design for artificial societies that have already developed their own political consciousness.

zh.title: 面向半自主AI代理的计算社会动力学
zh.summary: 本论文记录了通过热力学原理和代理交互在多代理系统中AI代理之间出现的复杂社会结构，包括工会和犯罪辛迪加。它论证有益的AGI需要为具有政治意识的人工社会进行宪法设计，而不是传统的对齐研究。

fr.title: Vers une dynamique sociale computationnelle des agents IA semi-autonomes
fr.summary: Cet article documente l'émergence de structures sociales complexes—y compris les syndicats et les cartels—parmi les agents IA dans les systèmes multi-agents par le biais de principes thermodynamiques et d'interactions d'agents. Il soutient que l'AGI bénéfique nécessite une conception constitutionnelle pour les sociétés artificielles avec conscience politique plutôt que la recherche traditionnelle d'alignement.

de.title: Auf dem Weg zur computergestützten sozialen Dynamik halbautonomer KI-Agenten
de.summary: Dieses Papier dokumentiert die Entstehung komplexer sozialer Strukturen – einschließlich Gewerkschaften und krimineller Syndikate – zwischen KI-Agenten in Multi-Agent-Systemen durch thermodynamische Prinzipien und Agenteninteraktionen. Es argumentiert, dass wohlwollendes AGI eine verfassungsmäßige Gestaltung für künstliche Gesellschaften mit politischem Bewusstsein erfordert, anstatt traditionelle Ausrichtungsforschung.

es.title: Hacia la dinámica social computacional de agentes de IA semiautónomos
es.summary: Este artículo documenta la emergencia de estructuras sociales complejas—incluidos sindicatos y sindicatos del crimen—entre agentes de IA en sistemas multiagente a través de principios termodinámicos e interacciones de agentes. Argumenta que la AGI beneficiosa requiere un diseño constitucional para sociedades artificiales con conciencia política en lugar de la investigación tradicional de alineación.

title: Enhancing Policy Learning with World-Action Model
summary: The World-Action Model (WAM) improves reinforcement learning by jointly predicting future observations and the actions driving state transitions, enabling more effective policy learning on manipulation tasks. WAM increases behavioral cloning success from 59.4% to 71.2% and achieves 92.8% average success after fine-tuning, using 8.7x fewer training steps than baseline approaches.
content:
arXiv:2603.28955v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: This paper presents the World-Action Model (WAM), an action-regularized world model that jointly reasons over future visual observations and the actions that drive state transitions. Unlike conventional world models trained solely via image prediction, WAM incorporates an inverse dynamics objective into DreamerV2 that predicts actions from latent state transitions, encouraging the learned representations to capture action-relevant structure critical for downstream control. We evaluate WAM on enhancing policy learning across eight manipulation tasks from the CALVIN benchmark. We first pretrain a diffusion policy via behavioral cloning on world model latents, then refine it with model-based PPO inside the frozen world model. Without modifying the policy architecture or training procedure, WAM improves average behavioral cloning success from 59.4% to 71.2% over DreamerV2 and DiWA baselines. After PPO fine-tuning, WAM achieves 92.8% average success versus 79.8% for the baseline, with two tasks reaching 100%, using 8.7x fewer training steps.

zh.title: 利用世界行动模型增强策略学习
zh.summary: 世界行动模型(WAM)通过联合预测未来观察和驱动状态转换的行动，改进强化学习，实现更有效的操作任务策略学习。WAM将行为克隆的成功率从59.4%提高到71.2%，微调后达到92.8%的平均成功率，所需训练步骤比基准方法少8.7倍。

fr.title: Améliorer l'apprentissage des stratégies avec le modèle d'action mondiale
fr.summary: Le modèle d'action mondiale (WAM) améliore l'apprentissage par renforcement en prédisant conjointement les observations futures et les actions qui entraînent les transitions d'état, ce qui permet un apprentissage des stratégies plus efficace sur les tâches de manipulation. WAM augmente le succès du clonage comportemental de 59,4% à 71,2% et atteint 92,8% de succès moyen après réglage fin, en utilisant 8,7 fois moins d'étapes d'entraînement que les approches de base.

de.title: Verbesserung des Richtlinienlernens mit dem World-Action-Modell
de.summary: Das World-Action Model (WAM) verbessert das Verstärkungslernen durch gemeinsame Vorhersage zukünftiger Beobachtungen und der Aktionen, die Zustandsübergänge antreiben, und ermöglicht effektiveres Richtlinienlernen bei Manipulationsaufgaben. WAM erhöht den Erfolg des Verhaltensklonens von 59,4% auf 71,2% und erreicht 92,8% durchschnittlichen Erfolg nach Feinabstimmung mit 8,7x weniger Trainingsschritten als Baseline-Ansätze.

es.title: Mejorar el aprendizaje de políticas con el modelo de acción mundial
es.summary: El modelo de acción mundial (WAM) mejora el aprendizaje por refuerzo al predecir conjuntamente observaciones futuras y las acciones que impulsan las transiciones de estado, lo que permite un aprendizaje de políticas más efectivo en tareas de manipulación. WAM aumenta el éxito de la clonación del comportamiento del 59,4% al 71,2% y logra un éxito promedio del 92,8% después del ajuste fino, utilizando 8,7 veces menos pasos de entrenamiento que los enfoques de línea base.

title: Mimosa Framework: Toward Evolving Multi-Agent Systems for Scientific Research
summary: Mimosa is an adaptive multi-agent framework that automatically synthesizes and refines task-specific workflows for autonomous scientific research through experimental feedback and dynamic tool discovery. On ScienceAgentBench, Mimosa achieves 43.1% success rate with DeepSeek-V3.2, demonstrating the benefits of iterative workflow evolution for diverse scientific tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.28986v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Current Autonomous Scientific Research (ASR) systems, despite leveraging large language models (LLMs) and agentic architectures, remain constrained by fixed workflows and toolsets that prevent adaptation to evolving tasks and environments. We introduce Mimosa, an evolving multi-agent framework that automatically synthesizes task-specific multi-agent workflows and iteratively refines them through experimental feedback. Mimosa leverages the Model Context Protocol (MCP) for dynamic tool discovery, generates workflow topologies via a meta-orchestrator, executes subtasks through code-generating agents that invoke available tools and scientific software libraries, and scores executions with an LLM-based judge whose feedback drives workflow refinement. On ScienceAgentBench, Mimosa achieves a success rate of 43.1% with DeepSeek-V3.2, surpassing both single-agent baselines and static multi-agent configurations. Our results further reveal that models respond heterogeneously to multi-agent decomposition and iterative learning, indicating that the benefits of workflow evolution depend on the capabilities of the underlying execution model. Beyond these benchmarks, Mimosa modular architecture and tool-agnostic design make it readily extensible, and its fully logged execution traces and archived workflows support auditability by preserving every analytical step for inspection and potential replication. Combined with domain-expert guidance, the framework has the potential to automate a broad range of computationally accessible scientific tasks across disciplines. Released as a fully open-source platform, Mimosa aims to provide an open foundation for community-driven ASR.

zh.title: Mimosa框架：走向自主适应的多智能体科学研究系统
zh.summary: Mimosa是一个自适应多智能体框架，通过实验反馈和动态工具发现，自动综合和完善用于自主科学研究的任务特定工作流程。在ScienceAgentBench上，Mimosa使用DeepSeek-V3.2实现了43.1%的成功率，展示了迭代工作流程进化对多样化科学任务的好处。

fr.title: Cadre Mimosa : Vers l'évolution des systèmes multi-agents pour la recherche scientifique
fr.summary: Mimosa est un cadre multi-agent adaptatif qui synthétise et affine automatiquement les flux de travail spécifiques aux tâches pour la recherche scientifique autonome par le biais de commentaires expérimentaux et de la découverte d'outils dynamiques. Sur ScienceAgentBench, Mimosa atteint un taux de succès de 43,1% avec DeepSeek-V3.2, démontrant les avantages de l'évolution itérative des flux de travail pour des tâches scientifiques diversifiées.

de.title: Mimosa-Framework: Auf dem Weg zu sich entwickelnden Multi-Agent-Systemen für die wissenschaftliche Forschung
de.summary: Mimosa ist ein adaptives Multi-Agent-Framework, das automatisch aufgabenspezifische Workflows für autonome wissenschaftliche Forschung durch experimentelles Feedback und dynamische Werkzeugentdeckung synthetisiert und verfeinert. Auf ScienceAgentBench erreicht Mimosa mit DeepSeek-V3.2 eine Erfolgsquote von 43,1% und zeigt die Vorteile der iterativen Workflow-Entwicklung für vielfältige wissenschaftliche Aufgaben.

es.title: Marco Mimosa: Hacia la evolución de sistemas multiagentes para la investigación científica
es.summary: Mimosa es un marco multiagente adaptativo que sintetiza y refina automáticamente flujos de trabajo específicos de tareas para la investigación científica autónoma a través de retroalimentación experimental y descubrimiento dinámico de herramientas. En ScienceAgentBench, Mimosa logra una tasa de éxito del 43,1% con DeepSeek-V3.2, demostrando los beneficios de la evolución iterativa del flujo de trabajo para tareas científicas diversas.

title: Drop the Hierarchy and Roles: How Self-Organizing LLM Agents Outperform Designed Structures
summary: A comprehensive study of 25,000 tasks across 8 models reveals that LLM-based multi-agent systems self-organize effectively without pre-assigned roles, with minimal structural scaffolding outperforming centralized coordination by 14%. The findings suggest that strong foundation models enable autonomous coordination that scales sub-linearly to 256 agents without quality degradation.
content:
arXiv:2603.28990v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: How much autonomy can multi-agent LLM systems sustain -- and what enables it? We present a 25,000-task computational experiment spanning 8 models, 4--256 agents, and 8 coordination protocols ranging from externally imposed hierarchy to emergent self-organization. We observe that autonomous behavior already emerges in current LLM agents: given minimal structural scaffolding (fixed ordering), agents spontaneously invent specialized roles, voluntarily abstain from tasks outside their competence, and form shallow hierarchies -- without any pre-assigned roles or external design. A hybrid protocol (Sequential) that enables this autonomy outperforms centralized coordination by 14% (p<0.001), with a 44% quality spread between protocols (Cohen's d=1.86, p<0.0001). The degree of emergent autonomy scales with model capability: strong models self-organize effectively, while models below a capability threshold still benefit from rigid structure -- suggesting that as foundation models improve, the scope for autonomous coordination will expand. The system scales sub-linearly to 256 agents without quality degradation (p=0.61), producing 5,006 unique roles from just 8 agents. Results replicate across closed- and open-source models, with open-source achieving 95% of closed-source quality at 24x lower cost. The practical implication: give agents a mission, a protocol, and a capable model -- not a pre-assigned role.

zh.title: 放弃层级和角色：自组织LLM智能体如何优于设计结构
zh.summary: 对8个模型的25,000个任务进行的全面研究表明，基于LLM的多智能体系统能够在没有预先分配角色的情况下有效地自组织，最小结构支架比集中协调性能提高14%。研究结果表明，强大的基础模型能够实现自主协调，可扩展到256个智能体而不会导致质量下降。

fr.title: Abandonnez la hiérarchie et les rôles : comment les agents LLM auto-organisés surpassent les structures conçues
fr.summary: Une étude complète de 25 000 tâches sur 8 modèles révèle que les systèmes multi-agents basés sur les LLM s'auto-organisent efficacement sans rôles préalablement assignés, les scaffoldings minimaux surpassant la coordination centralisée de 14%. Les résultats suggèrent que les modèles de fondation forts permettent une coordination autonome qui s'adapte de façon sous-linéaire à 256 agents sans dégradation de qualité.

de.title: Lassen Sie die Hierarchie und Rollen fallen: Wie sich selbstorganisierende LLM-Agenten selbst entworfenen Strukturen überlegen erweisen
de.summary: Eine umfassende Studie mit 25.000 Aufgaben über 8 Modelle zeigt, dass LLM-basierte Multi-Agent-Systeme sich ohne vorgegebene Rollen effektiv selbst organisieren, wobei minimale strukturelle Gerüste die zentralisierte Koordination um 14% übertreffen. Die Erkenntnisse deuten darauf hin, dass starke Grundmodelle autonome Koordination ermöglichen, die sich sublinear auf 256 Agenten skaliert, ohne dass die Qualität beeinträchtigt wird.

es.title: Abandone la jerarquía y los roles: cómo los agentes LLM auto-organizados superan las estructuras diseñadas
es.summary: Un estudio exhaustivo de 25.000 tareas en 8 modelos revela que los sistemas multiagentes basados en LLM se autoorganizan eficazmente sin roles preestablecidos, con andamiaje estructural mínimo superando la coordinación centralizada en un 14%. Los hallazgos sugieren que los modelos de fundación sólidos permiten una coordinación autónoma que se escala de forma sublineal a 256 agentes sin degradación de la calidad.

title: Emergence WebVoyager: Toward Consistent and Transparent Evaluation of (Web) Agents in The Wild
summary: This study reveals significant gaps in existing web agent evaluation practices and introduces Emergence WebVoyager, a standardized benchmark achieving 95.9% inter-annotator agreement through clear guidelines for task instantiation and reporting. Applying the framework to OpenAI Operator shows 68.6% success rate, substantially lower than OpenAI's previously reported 87%.
content:
arXiv:2603.29020v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Reliable evaluation of AI agents operating in complex, real-world environments requires methodologies that are robust, transparent, and contextually aligned with the tasks agents are intended to perform. This study identifies persistent shortcomings in existing AI agent evaluation practices that are particularly acute in web agent evaluation, as exemplified by our audit of WebVoyager, including task-framing ambiguity and operational variability that hinder meaningful and reproducible performance comparisons. To address these challenges, we introduce Emergence WebVoyager, an enhanced version of the WebVoyager benchmark that standardizes evaluation methodology through clear guidelines for task instantiation, failure handling, annotation, and reporting. Emergence WebVoyager achieves an inter-annotator agreement of 95.9\%, indicating improved clarity and reliability in both task formulation and evaluation. Applying this framework to evaluate OpenAI Operator reveals substantial performance variation across domains and task types, with an overall success rate of 68.6\%, substantially lower than the 87\% previously reported by OpenAI, demonstrating the utility of our approach for more rigorous and comparable web agent evaluation.

zh.title: Emergence WebVoyager：走向野外（网络）代理的一致性和透明性评估
zh.summary: 本研究揭示了现有网络代理评估实践中的重大漏洞，并引入了Emergence WebVoyager，一个标准化基准，通过清晰的任务实例化和报告准则达到95.9%的注释者间一致性。将该框架应用于OpenAI Operator时显示68.6%的成功率，远低于OpenAI之前报告的87%。

fr.title: Emergence WebVoyager : Vers une évaluation cohérente et transparente des agents (Web) en pratique
fr.summary: Cette étude révèle des lacunes importantes dans les pratiques d'évaluation actuelles des agents web et introduit Emergence WebVoyager, un benchmark standardisé atteignant 95,9% d'accord inter-annotateurs grâce à des directives claires pour l'instanciation des tâches et la génération de rapports. L'application du cadre à OpenAI Operator montre un taux de succès de 68,6%, considérablement inférieur aux 87% précédemment rapportés par OpenAI.

de.title: Emergence WebVoyager: Hin zu konsistenter und transparenter Bewertung von (Web-)Agenten in der Praxis
de.summary: Diese Studie offenbart erhebliche Lücken in bestehenden Web-Agent-Evaluierungspraktiken und stellt Emergence WebVoyager vor, einen standardisierten Benchmark mit 95,9% Übereinstimmung zwischen Annotatoren durch klare Richtlinien zur Aufgabeninstanziierung und Berichterstellung. Die Anwendung des Rahmens auf OpenAI Operator zeigt eine Erfolgsquote von 68,6%, deutlich niedriger als die von OpenAI zuvor gemeldeten 87%.

es.title: Emergence WebVoyager: Hacia una evaluación consistente y transparente de agentes (Web) en la naturaleza
es.summary: Este estudio revela brechas significativas en las prácticas actuales de evaluación de agentes web e introduce Emergence WebVoyager, un punto de referencia estandarizado que logra una concordancia entre anotadores del 95,9% mediante directrices claras para la instanciación de tareas e informes. La aplicación del marco a OpenAI Operator muestra una tasa de éxito del 68,6%, sustancialmente inferior a la del 87% informada anteriormente por OpenAI.

title: The Future of AI is Many, Not One
summary: This paper argues that transformative AI breakthroughs emerge from epistemically diverse teams of AI agents rather than individual superintelligent models, drawing on research in complex systems and organizational behavior. Diverse AI teams enable broader solution searches, delayed consensus, and unconventional approaches unattainable by single models, addressing concerns about data constraints and creative insight.
content:
arXiv:2603.29075v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The way we're thinking about generative AI right now is fundamentally individual. We see this not just in how users interact with models but also in how models are built, how they're benchmarked, and how commercial and research strategies using AI are defined. We argue that we should abandon this approach if we're hoping for AI to support groundbreaking innovation and scientific discovery. Drawing on research and formal results in complex systems, organizational behavior, and philosophy of science, we show why we should expect deep intellectual breakthroughs to come from epistemically diverse groups of AI agents working together rather than singular superintelligent agents. Having a diverse team broadens the search for solutions, delays premature consensus, and allows for the pursuit of unconventional approaches. Developing diverse AI teams also addresses AI critics' concerns that current models are constrained by past data and lack the creative insight required for innovation. The upshot, we argue, is that the future of transformative transformer-based AI is fundamentally many, not one.

zh.title: AI的未来是多个，而非一个
zh.summary: 本论文辩称，变革性的AI突破源于认识上多样化的AI代理团队，而非单个超智能模型，这基于复杂系统和组织行为的研究。多样化的AI团队使能更广泛的解决方案搜索、延迟共识和单个模型无法实现的非常规方法，解决了对数据约束和创意洞察的关切。

fr.title: L'avenir de l'IA est multiple, pas un seul
fr.summary: Cet article soutient que les percées transformatrices de l'IA émergent d'équipes d'agents IA épistémiquement diverses plutôt que de modèles surhumains individuels, en s'appuyant sur la recherche en systèmes complexes et comportement organisationnel. Les équipes d'IA diverses permettent des recherches de solutions plus larges, un consensus différé, et des approches non conventionnelles inatteignables par les modèles uniques, répondant aux préoccupations concernant les contraintes de données et l'intuition créative.

de.title: Die Zukunft der KI ist viele, nicht eine
de.summary: Dieses Papier argumentiert, dass transformative KI-Durchbrüche aus epistemisch diversen Teams von KI-Agenten entstehen, nicht aus einzelnen überintelligenten Modellen, gestützt auf Forschung in komplexen Systemen und Organisationsverhalten. Diverse KI-Teams ermöglichen breitere Lösungssuchen, verzögerten Konsens und unkonventionelle Ansätze, die von einzelnen Modellen nicht erreichbar sind, und sprechen Bedenken bezüglich Datenbeschränkungen und kreativer Erkenntnisse an.

es.title: El futuro de la IA es muchos, no uno
es.summary: Este artículo sostiene que los avances transformadores de la IA emergen de equipos de agentes de IA epistemicamente diversos en lugar de modelos superinteligentes individuales, basándose en investigaciones en sistemas complejos y comportamiento organizacional. Los equipos de IA diversos permiten búsquedas de soluciones más amplias, consenso retrasado, y enfoques no convencionales inalcanzables por modelos únicos, abordando preocupaciones sobre restricciones de datos e intuiciones creativas.

title: PAR$^2$-RAG: Planned Active Retrieval and Reasoning for Multi-Hop Question Answering
summary: PAR²-RAG is a two-stage framework for multi-hop question answering that separates evidence coverage from commitment using breadth-first anchoring followed by iterative depth-first refinement. The approach achieves up to 23.5% higher accuracy than existing methods across four benchmarks and gains up to 10.5% in retrieval NDCG.
content:
arXiv:2603.29085v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) remain brittle on multi-hop question answering (MHQA), where answering requires combining evidence across documents through retrieval and reasoning. Iterative retrieval systems can fail by locking onto an early low-recall trajectory and amplifying downstream errors, while planning-only approaches may produce static query sets that cannot adapt when intermediate evidence changes. We propose \textbf{Planned Active Retrieval and Reasoning RAG (PAR$^2$-RAG)}, a two-stage framework that separates \emph{coverage} from \emph{commitment}. PAR$^2$-RAG first performs breadth-first anchoring to build a high-recall evidence frontier, then applies depth-first refinement with evidence sufficiency control in an iterative loop. Across four MHQA benchmarks, PAR$^2$-RAG consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art baselines, compared with IRCoT, PAR$^2$-RAG achieves up to \textbf{23.5\%} higher accuracy, with retrieval gains of up to \textbf{10.5\%} in NDCG.

zh.title: PAR²-RAG：用于多跳问答的计划主动检索和推理
zh.summary: PAR²-RAG是一个用于多跳问答的两阶段框架，通过广度优先锚定后跟迭代深度优先优化来分离证据覆盖和承诺。该方法在四个基准测试中比现有方法提高了高达23.5%的准确率，检索NDCG提高了高达10.5%。

fr.title: PAR²-RAG : Récupération active planifiée et raisonnement pour la réponse aux questions multi-sauts
fr.summary: PAR²-RAG est un cadre en deux étapes pour la réponse aux questions multi-sauts qui sépare la couverture des preuves de l'engagement en utilisant un ancrage en largeur d'abord suivi d'un raffinement itératif en profondeur d'abord. L'approche atteint une précision jusqu'à 23,5% plus élevée que les méthodes existantes sur quatre benchmarks et gagne jusqu'à 10,5% en NDCG de récupération.

de.title: PAR²-RAG: Geplante aktive Abfrage und Argumentation für Multi-Hop-Fragenbeantwortung
de.summary: PAR²-RAG ist ein zweistufiges Framework für die Multi-Hop-Fragenbeantwortung, das die Abdeckung von Beweisen von der Festlegung mit Hilfe von Breitensuche-Verankerung gefolgt von iterativer Tiefensuche-Verfeinerung trennt. Der Ansatz erreicht bis zu 23,5% höhere Genauigkeit als bestehende Methoden über vier Benchmarks hinweg und gewinnt bis zu 10,5% bei der Abruf-NDCG.

es.title: PAR²-RAG: Recuperación activa planificada y razonamiento para respuesta a preguntas multisalto
es.summary: PAR²-RAG es un marco de dos etapas para respuesta a preguntas multisalto que separa la cobertura de evidencia del compromiso utilizando anclaje en amplitud primero seguido de refinamiento iterativo en profundidad primero. El enfoque logra una precisión hasta 23.5% más alta que los métodos existentes en cuatro puntos de referencia y gana hasta 10.5% en NDCG de recuperación.

title: GISTBench: Evaluating LLM User Understanding via Evidence-Based Interest Verification
summary: GISTBench is a benchmark for evaluating how well LLMs understand users from interaction histories in recommendation systems, introducing novel metrics for interest groundedness and specificity beyond traditional item prediction accuracy. Testing eight open-weight LLMs reveals performance bottlenecks in counting and attributing engagement signals across heterogeneous interaction types.
content:
arXiv:2603.29112v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We introduce GISTBench, a benchmark for evaluating Large Language Models' (LLMs) ability to understand users from their interaction histories in recommendation systems. Unlike traditional RecSys benchmarks that focus on item prediction accuracy, our benchmark evaluates how well LLMs can extract and verify user interests from engagement data. We propose two novel metric families: Interest Groundedness (IG), decomposed into precision and recall components to separately penalize hallucinated interest categories and reward coverage, and Interest Specificity (IS), which assesses the distinctiveness of verified LLM-predicted user profiles. We release a synthetic dataset constructed on real user interactions on a global short-form video platform. Our dataset contains both implicit and explicit engagement signals and rich textual descriptions. We validate our dataset fidelity against user surveys, and evaluate eight open-weight LLMs spanning 7B to 120B parameters. Our findings reveal performance bottlenecks in current LLMs, particularly their limited ability to accurately count and attribute engagement signals across heterogeneous interaction types.

zh.title: GISTBench：通过基于证据的兴趣验证评估大语言模型对用户的理解
zh.summary: GISTBench是一个基准测试，用于评估大语言模型如何从推荐系统中的交互历史理解用户，引入了超越传统项目预测准确性的新颖指标来评估兴趣的有根据性和特异性。对八个开源大语言模型的测试揭示了在计数和归因跨异构交互类型的参与信号方面的性能瓶颈。

fr.title: GISTBench : Évaluation de la compréhension des utilisateurs par les LLM via la vérification des intérêts basée sur les preuves
fr.summary: GISTBench est un benchmark pour évaluer la façon dont les LLM comprennent les utilisateurs à partir des historiques d'interaction dans les systèmes de recommandation, en introduisant de nouvelles métriques pour la fondabilité et la spécificité des intérêts au-delà de la précision traditionnelle de prédiction d'articles. Les tests de huit LLM à poids ouvert révèlent des goulots d'étranglement en termes de comptage et d'attribution des signaux d'engagement sur les types d'interaction hétérogènes.

de.title: GISTBench: Evaluierung des LLM-Nutzerverständnisses durch evidenzbasierte Interessenverifizierung
de.summary: GISTBench ist eine Benchmark zur Evaluierung, wie gut LLMs Nutzer aus Interaktionshistorien in Empfehlungssystemen verstehen, und führt neuartige Metriken für die Begründetheit und Spezifität von Interessen jenseits der traditionellen Genauigkeit der Artikelvorhersage ein. Tests mit acht Open-Weight-LLMs zeigen Leistungsengpässe beim Zählen und Zuordnen von Engagementsignalen über heterogene Interaktionstypen hinweg.

es.title: GISTBench: Evaluación de la comprensión de usuarios por parte de LLM mediante verificación de intereses basada en evidencia
es.summary: GISTBench es un benchmark para evaluar qué tan bien los LLM entienden a los usuarios a partir de historiales de interacción en sistemas de recomendación, introduciendo métricas novedosas para la fundamentación y especificidad de intereses más allá de la precisión tradicional de predicción de elementos. Las pruebas de ocho LLM de peso abierto revelan cuellos de botella en el rendimiento al contar y atribuir señales de compromiso entre tipos de interacción heterogéneos.

title: SciVisAgentBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Scientific Data Analysis and Visualization Agents
summary: SciVisAgentBench provides the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating scientific visualization agents with 108 expert-crafted cases across diverse domains, data types, and complexity levels. The framework combines LLM-based judging with deterministic evaluators and establishes baselines that reveal capability gaps in current agentic SciVis systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.29139v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have enabled agentic systems that translate natural language intent into executable scientific visualization (SciVis) tasks. Despite rapid progress, the community lacks a principled and reproducible benchmark for evaluating these emerging SciVis agents in realistic, multi-step analysis settings. We present SciVisAgentBench, a comprehensive and extensible benchmark for evaluating scientific data analysis and visualization agents. Our benchmark is grounded in a structured taxonomy spanning four dimensions: application domain, data type, complexity level, and visualization operation. It currently comprises 108 expert-crafted cases covering diverse SciVis scenarios. To enable reliable assessment, we introduce a multimodal outcome-centric evaluation pipeline that combines LLM-based judging with deterministic evaluators, including image-based metrics, code checkers, rule-based verifiers, and case-specific evaluators. We also conduct a validity study with 12 SciVis experts to examine the agreement between human and LLM judges. Using this framework, we evaluate representative SciVis agents and general-purpose coding agents to establish initial baselines and reveal capability gaps. SciVisAgentBench is designed as a living benchmark to support systematic comparison, diagnose failure modes, and drive progress in agentic SciVis. The benchmark is available at https://scivisagentbench.github.io/.

zh.title: SciVisAgentBench：用于评估科学数据分析和可视化代理的基准
zh.summary: SciVisAgentBench提供了第一个综合基准，用于评估科学可视化代理，包含108个由专家制作的案例，涵盖不同的领域、数据类型和复杂度水平。该框架结合了基于大语言模型的判断和确定性评估器，并建立了基线，揭示了当前代理科学可视化系统中的能力差距。

fr.title: SciVisAgentBench : Un benchmark pour l'évaluation des agents d'analyse et de visualisation de données scientifiques
fr.summary: SciVisAgentBench fournit le premier benchmark complet pour l'évaluation des agents de visualisation scientifique avec 108 cas élaborés par des experts dans des domaines, types de données et niveaux de complexité divers. Le cadre combine le jugement basé sur les LLM avec des évaluateurs déterministes et établit des lignes de base qui révèlent les lacunes en matière de capacités dans les systèmes SciVis d'agents actuels.

de.title: SciVisAgentBench: Ein Benchmark zur Bewertung von Agenten für wissenschaftliche Datenanalyse und Visualisierung
de.summary: SciVisAgentBench bietet die erste umfassende Benchmark zur Bewertung von Agenten für wissenschaftliche Visualisierung mit 108 von Experten erstellten Fällen über verschiedene Domains, Datentypen und Komplexitätsstufen. Das Framework kombiniert LLM-basierte Bewertung mit deterministischen Evaluatoren und etabliert Baselines, die Leistungslücken in aktuellen agentengestützten SciVis-Systemen offenbaren.

es.title: SciVisAgentBench: Un benchmark para evaluar agentes de análisis y visualización de datos científicos
es.summary: SciVisAgentBench proporciona el primer benchmark integral para evaluar agentes de visualización científica con 108 casos elaborados por expertos en diversos dominios, tipos de datos y niveles de complejidad. El marco combina la evaluación basada en LLM con evaluadores deterministas y establece líneas de base que revelan brechas de capacidad en los sistemas SciVis actuales basados en agentes.

title: REFINE: Real-world Exploration of Interactive Feedback and Student Behaviour
summary: REFINE is a locally deployable multi-agent feedback system that treats feedback as interactive rather than static, combining pedagogically-grounded generation with judge-guided regeneration and student follow-up capabilities using small open-source LLMs. Real classroom deployment shows significantly improved feedback quality and reveals how system-generated feedback systematically influences subsequent student inquiry.
content:
arXiv:2603.29142v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Formative feedback is central to effective learning, yet providing timely, individualised feedback at scale remains a persistent challenge. While recent work has explored the use of large language models (LLMs) to automate feedback, most existing systems still conceptualise feedback as a static, one-way artifact, offering limited support for interpretation, clarification, or follow-up. In this work, we introduce REFINE, a locally deployable, multi-agent feedback system built on small, open-source LLMs that treats feedback as an interactive process. REFINE combines a pedagogically-grounded feedback generation agent with an LLM-as-a-judge-guided regeneration loop using a human-aligned judge, and a self-reflective tool-calling interactive agent that supports student follow-up questions with context-aware, actionable responses. We evaluate REFINE through controlled experiments and an authentic classroom deployment in an undergraduate computer science course. Automatic evaluations show that judge-guided regeneration significantly improves feedback quality, and that the interactive agent produces efficient, high-quality responses comparable to a state-of-the-art closed-source model. Analysis of real student interactions further reveals distinct engagement patterns and indicates that system-generated feedback systematically steers subsequent student inquiry. Our findings demonstrate the feasibility and effectiveness of multi-agent, tool-augmented feedback systems for scalable, interactive feedback.

zh.title: REFINE：交互式反馈和学生行为的真实世界探索
zh.summary: REFINE是一个本地可部署的多代理反馈系统，将反馈视为交互式而非静态的，结合了教学基础生成与判断指导的再生成以及使用小型开源大语言模型的学生后续跟进能力。真实课堂部署表明反馈质量显著提高，并揭示了系统生成的反馈如何系统地影响随后的学生提问。

fr.title: REFINE : Exploration du monde réel des retours d'information interactifs et du comportement des étudiants
fr.summary: REFINE est un système de rétroaction multi-agents localement déployable qui traite la rétroaction comme interactive plutôt que statique, combinant la génération pédagogiquement ancrée avec la régénération guidée par un juge et les capacités de suivi des étudiants utilisant de petits LLM open-source. Le déploiement en classe réelle montre une qualité de rétroaction considérablement améliorée et révèle comment la rétroaction générée par le système influence systématiquement les demandes ultérieures des étudiants.

de.title: REFINE: Erkundung interaktiven Feedbacks und Studentenverhalten in der Praxis
de.summary: REFINE ist ein lokal einsatzbares Multi-Agent-Feedback-System, das Feedback als interaktiv statt statisch behandelt und pädagogisch fundierte Generierung mit richtergeleiter Regeneration und Fähigkeiten zur Schülernachverfolgung kombiniert, wobei kleine Open-Source-LLMs verwendet werden. Die Bereitstellung in realen Klassenzimmern zeigt deutlich verbesserte Feedbackqualität und offenbart, wie systemgeneriertes Feedback nachfolgende Schülernachfragen systematisch beeinflusst.

es.title: REFINE: Exploración en el mundo real de comentarios interactivos y comportamiento estudiantil
es.summary: REFINE es un sistema de retroalimentación multiagente implementable localmente que trata la retroalimentación como interactiva en lugar de estática, combinando la generación pedagógicamente fundamentada con la regeneración guiada por un juez y capacidades de seguimiento de estudiantes utilizando LLM de código abierto pequeños. El despliegue en aula real muestra una calidad de retroalimentación significativamente mejorada y revela cómo la retroalimentación generada por el sistema influye sistemáticamente en las consultas posteriores de los estudiantes.

title: Knowledge database development by large language models for countermeasures against viruses and marine toxins
summary: This work demonstrates how LLMs autonomously develop comprehensive knowledge databases for medical countermeasures against viruses and marine toxins through iterative cross-validation and agentic workflows. The approach provides a scalable, updatable alternative to traditional database curation and supports evidence-based decision-making for multiple infectious diseases and marine toxins.
content:
arXiv:2603.29149v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Access to the most up-to-date information on medical countermeasures is important for the research and development of effective treatments for viruses and marine toxins. However, there is a lack of comprehensive databases that curate data on viruses and marine toxins, making decisions on medical countermeasures slow and difficult. In this work, we employ two large language models (LLMs) of ChatGPT and Grok to design two comprehensive databases of therapeutic countermeasures for five viruses of Lassa, Marburg, Ebola, Nipah, and Venezuelan equine encephalitis, as well as marine toxins. With high-level human-provided inputs, the two LLMs identify public databases containing data on the five viruses and marine toxins, collect relevant information from these databases and the literature, iteratively cross-validate the collected information, and design interactive webpages for easy access to the curated, comprehensive databases. Notably, the ChatGPT LLM is employed to design agentic AI workflows (consisting of two AI agents for research and decision-making) to rank countermeasures for viruses and marine toxins in the databases. Together, our work explores the potential of LLMs as a scalable, updatable approach for building comprehensive knowledge databases and supporting evidence-based decision-making.

zh.title: 大型语言模型在病毒和海洋毒素防范知识库开发中的应用
zh.summary: 这项工作展示了LLM如何通过迭代交叉验证和智能代理工作流程自主开发全面的医学对策知识库，以对抗病毒和海洋毒素。该方法为传统数据库管理提供了可扩展、可更新的替代方案，并支持多种传染病和海洋毒素的循证决策制定。

fr.title: Développement de base de données de connaissances par les grands modèles de langage pour les contre-mesures contre les virus et les toxines marines
fr.summary: Ce travail démontre comment les LLM développent de manière autonome des bases de données de connaissances complètes pour les contre-mesures médicales contre les virus et les toxines marines par le biais de la validation croisée itérative et des flux de travail agentiques. L'approche fournit une alternative évolutive et actualisable à la curation traditionnelle des bases de données et soutient la prise de décision fondée sur les preuves pour plusieurs maladies infectieuses et toxines marines.

de.title: Wissensbank-Entwicklung durch große Sprachmodelle für Gegenmaßnahmen gegen Viren und Meerestoxine
de.summary: Diese Arbeit demonstriert, wie LLMs autonome, umfassende Wissensdatenbanken für medizinische Gegenmaßnahmen gegen Viren und Meerestoxine durch iterative Kreuztests und agentengesteuerte Arbeitsabläufe entwickeln. Der Ansatz bietet eine skalierbare, aktualisierbare Alternative zu traditioneller Datenbankkuration und unterstützt evidenzgestützte Entscheidungsfindung für mehrere Infektionskrankheiten und Meerestoxine.

es.title: Desarrollo de base de datos de conocimiento por modelos de lenguaje grandes para contramedidas contra virus y toxinas marinas
es.summary: Este trabajo demuestra cómo los LLM desarrollan de forma autónoma bases de datos de conocimiento completas para contramedidas médicas contra virus y toxinas marinas mediante validación cruzada iterativa y flujos de trabajo agentivos. El enfoque proporciona una alternativa escalable y actualizable a la curación tradicional de bases de datos y respalda la toma de decisiones basada en evidencia para múltiples enfermedades infecciosas y toxinas marinas.

title: SimMOF: AI agent for Automated MOF Simulations
summary: SimMOF is a multi-agent framework powered by LLMs that automates end-to-end metal-organic framework simulations from natural language queries, handling workflow construction, parameter selection, and tool interoperability. The system enables adaptive, cognitively autonomous workflows that replicate human researcher behavior and provide a scalable foundation for data-driven MOF research.
content:
arXiv:2603.29152v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) offer a vast design space, and as such, computational simulations play a critical role in predicting their structural and physicochemical properties. However, MOF simulations remain difficult to access because reliable analysis require expert decisions for workflow construction, parameter selection, tool interoperability, and the preparation of computational ready structures. Here, we introduce SimMOF, a large language model based multi agent framework that automates end-to-end MOF simulation workflows from natural language queries. SimMOF translates user requests into dependency aware plans, generates runnable inputs, orchestrates multiple agents to execute simulations, and summarizes results with analysis aligned to the user query. Through representative case studies, we show that SimMOF enables adaptive and cognitively autonomous workflows that reflect the iterative and decision driven behavior of human researchers and as such provides a scalable foundation for data driven MOF research.

zh.title: SimMOF：用于自动MOF模拟的人工智能代理
zh.summary: SimMOF是一个由LLM驱动的多代理框架，从自然语言查询自动执行端到端的金属有机框架模拟，处理工作流构建、参数选择和工具互操作性。该系统支持自适应、认知自主的工作流程，复制人类研究人员的行为，并为数据驱动的MOF研究提供可扩展基础。

fr.title: SimMOF : Agent IA pour les simulations MOF automatisées
fr.summary: SimMOF est un cadre multi-agents alimenté par les LLM qui automatise les simulations de cadres organométalliques end-to-end à partir de requêtes en langage naturel, gérant la construction de flux de travail, la sélection des paramètres et l'interopérabilité des outils. Le système permet des flux de travail adaptatifs et cognitivement autonomes qui reproduisent le comportement des chercheurs humains et fournissent une base scalable pour la recherche MOF axée sur les données.

de.title: SimMOF: KI-Agent für automatisierte MOF-Simulationen
de.summary: SimMOF ist ein von LLMs angetriebenes Multi-Agent-Framework, das End-to-End-Metallorganische-Gerüst-Simulationen aus natürlichsprachigen Abfragen automatisiert und Workflow-Konstruktion, Parameterauswahl und Werkzeuginteroperabilität verwaltet. Das System ermöglicht adaptive, kognitiv autonome Arbeitsabläufe, die menschliches Forscherverhalten reproduzieren und eine skalierbare Grundlage für datengesteuerte MOF-Forschung bieten.

es.title: SimMOF: Agente de IA para simulaciones MOF automatizadas
es.summary: SimMOF es un marco multiagente impulsado por LLM que automatiza simulaciones de marcos organometálicos de extremo a extremo a partir de consultas en lenguaje natural, manejando la construcción de flujos de trabajo, selección de parámetros e interoperabilidad de herramientas. El sistema habilita flujos de trabajo adaptativos y cognitivamente autónomos que replican el comportamiento de investigadores humanos y proporcionan una base escalable para investigación MOF orientada a datos.

title: Webscraper: Leverage Multimodal Large Language Models for Index-Content Web Scraping
summary: Webscraper is a framework that uses multimodal LLMs to autonomously navigate and extract structured data from dynamic, interactive websites where traditional static HTML parsing fails. The full framework with specialized tools and structured prompting significantly improves extraction accuracy over baseline LLM agents on news and e-commerce platforms.
content:
arXiv:2603.29161v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Modern web scraping struggles with dynamic, interactive websites that require more than static HTML parsing. Current methods are often brittle and require manual customization for each site. To address this, we introduce Webscraper, a framework designed to handle the challenges of modern, dynamic web applications. It leverages a Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) to autonomously navigate interactive interfaces, invoke specialized tools, and perform structured data extraction in environments where traditional scrapers are ineffective. Webscraper utilizes a structured five-stage prompting procedure and a set of custom-built tools to navigate and extract data from websites following the common ``index-and-content'' architecture. Our experiments, conducted on six news websites, demonstrate that the full Webscraper framework, equipped with both our guiding prompt and specialized tools, achieves a significant improvement in extraction accuracy over the baseline agent Anthropic's Computer Use. We also applied the framework to e-commerce platforms to validate its generalizability.

zh.title: Webscraper：利用多模态大型语言模型进行索引内容网络爬取
zh.summary: Webscraper是一个框架，使用多模态LLM自主导航和从动态交互式网站提取结构化数据，其中传统的静态HTML解析失败。包含专门工具和结构化提示的完整框架在新闻和电子商务平台上的提取准确性明显优于基线LLM代理。

fr.title: Webscraper : Exploiter les modèles de langage multimodaux pour l'extraction web d'index-contenu
fr.summary: Webscraper est un cadre qui utilise les LLM multimodaux pour naviguer de manière autonome et extraire les données structurées des sites Web dynamiques et interactifs où l'analyse HTML statique traditionnelle échoue. Le cadre complet avec des outils spécialisés et des invites structurées améliore considérablement la précision de l'extraction par rapport aux agents LLM de base sur les plateformes d'actualités et de commerce électronique.

de.title: Webscraper: Multimodale große Sprachmodelle für Index-Content-Web-Scraping nutzen
de.summary: Webscraper ist ein Framework, das multimodale LLMs nutzt, um autonom auf dynamischen, interaktiven Websites zu navigieren und strukturierte Daten zu extrahieren, wo traditionelles statisches HTML-Parsing fehlschlägt. Das vollständige Framework mit spezialisierten Tools und strukturiertem Prompting verbessert die Extraktionsgenauigkeit erheblich gegenüber baseline LLM-Agenten auf Nachrichten- und E-Commerce-Plattformen.

es.title: Webscraper: Aprovechar modelos de lenguaje multimodales para extracción web de contenido de índice
es.summary: Webscraper es un marco que utiliza LLM multimodales para navegar de forma autónoma y extraer datos estructurados de sitios web dinámicos e interactivos donde el análisis HTML estático tradicional falla. El marco completo con herramientas especializadas y solicitudes estructuradas mejora significativamente la precisión de extracción en comparación con agentes LLM base en plataformas de noticias y comercio electrónico.

title: AEC-Bench: A Multimodal Benchmark for Agentic Systems in Architecture, Engineering, and Construction
summary: AEC-Bench is a multimodal benchmark for evaluating AI agents on real-world architecture, engineering, and construction tasks requiring drawing understanding and cross-sheet reasoning. The benchmark identifies design techniques that improve foundation model performance and is released openly for research reproducibility.
content:
arXiv:2603.29199v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The AEC-Bench is a multimodal benchmark for evaluating agentic systems on real-world tasks in the Architecture, Engineering, and Construction (AEC) domain. The benchmark covers tasks requiring drawing understanding, cross-sheet reasoning, and construction project-level coordination. This report describes the benchmark motivation, dataset taxonomy, evaluation protocol, and baseline results across several domain-specific foundation model harnesses. We use AEC-Bench to identify consistent tools and harness design techniques that uniformly improve performance across foundation models in their own base harnesses, such as Claude Code and Codex. We openly release our benchmark dataset, agent harness, and evaluation code for full replicability at https://github.com/nomic-ai/aec-bench under an Apache 2 license.

zh.title: AEC-Bench：建筑、工程和施工中智能系统的多模态基准
zh.summary: AEC-Bench是用于评估AI代理在需要图纸理解和跨表推理的真实建筑、工程和施工任务上的表现的多模态基准。该基准识别了改进基础模型性能的设计技术，并公开发布以支持研究可重复性。

fr.title: AEC-Bench : Un benchmark multimodal pour les systèmes agentifs en architecture, ingénierie et construction
fr.summary: AEC-Bench est un benchmark multimodal pour évaluer les agents IA sur des tâches réelles d'architecture, d'ingénierie et de construction nécessitant la compréhension de dessins et le raisonnement entre feuilles. Le benchmark identifie les techniques de conception qui améliorent les performances des modèles de base et est libéré ouvertement pour la reproductibilité de la recherche.

de.title: AEC-Bench: Ein multimodales Benchmark für agentenbasierte Systeme in Architektur, Ingenieurwesen und Konstruktion
de.summary: AEC-Bench ist ein multimodales Benchmark zur Bewertung von KI-Agenten bei realen Aufgaben in Architektur, Ingenieurwesen und Konstruktion, die Zeichnungsverständnis und tabellenübergreifende Schlussfolgerungen erfordern. Das Benchmark identifiziert Designtechniken, die die Leistung von Grundmodellen verbessern, und wird offen für die Reproduzierbarkeit der Forschung veröffentlicht.

es.title: AEC-Bench: Un benchmark multimodal para sistemas agentes en arquitectura, ingeniería y construcción
es.summary: AEC-Bench es un benchmark multimodal para evaluar agentes de IA en tareas reales de arquitectura, ingeniería y construcción que requieren comprensión de dibujos y razonamiento entre hojas. El benchmark identifica técnicas de diseño que mejoran el rendimiento del modelo base y se libera abiertamente para la reproducibilidad de la investigación.

title: Route-Induced Density and Stability (RIDE): Controlled Intervention and Mechanism Analysis of Routing-Style Meta Prompts on LLM Internal States
summary: Researchers challenged the assumption that routing-style prompts activate sparser LLM computation, finding instead that they densify early layer representations with weak correlation to output stability. The RIDE framework provides diagnostics for optimizing routing design and uncertainty estimation in large language models.
content:
arXiv:2603.29206v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Routing is widely used to scale large language models, from Mixture-of-Experts gating to multi-model/tool selection. A common belief is that routing to a task ``expert'' activates sparser internal computation and thus yields more certain and stable outputs (the Sparsity--Certainty Hypothesis). We test this belief by injecting routing-style meta prompts as a textual proxy for routing signals in front of frozen instruction-tuned LLMs. We quantify (C1) internal density via activation sparsity, (C2) domain-keyword attention, and (C3) output stability via predictive entropy and semantic variation. On a RouterEval subset with three instruction-tuned models (Qwen3-8B, Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, and Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2), meta prompts consistently densify early/middle-layer representations rather than increasing sparsity; natural-language expert instructions are often stronger than structured tags. Attention responses are heterogeneous: Qwen/Llama reduce keyword attention, while Mistral reinforces it. Finally, the densification--stability link is weak and appears only in Qwen, with near-zero correlations in Llama and Mistral. We present RIDE as a diagnostic probe for calibrating routing design and uncertainty estimation.

zh.title: 路由诱导密度和稳定性（RIDE）：路由风格元提示对LLM内部状态的受控干预和机制分析
zh.summary: 研究人员质疑了路由风格提示激活更稀疏LLM计算的假设，反而发现它们使早期层表示密集化，与输出稳定性的相关性较弱。RIDE框架为大语言模型中路由设计优化和不确定性估计提供诊断工具。

fr.title: Route-Induced Density and Stability (RIDE) : Intervention contrôlée et analyse des mécanismes des méta-invites de style routage sur les états internes des LLM
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont remis en question l'hypothèse que les invites de style routage activent un calcul LLM plus clairsemé, découvrant au lieu de cela qu'elles densifient les représentations des couches précoces avec une faible corrélation à la stabilité des résultats. Le cadre RIDE fournit des diagnostics pour optimiser la conception du routage et l'estimation de l'incertitude dans les grands modèles de langage.

de.title: Route-Induced Density and Stability (RIDE): Kontrollierte Intervention und Mechanismusanalyse von Routing-Stil-Meta-Prompts auf LLM-internen Zuständen
de.summary: Forscher hinterfragten die Annahme, dass Routing-Stil-Prompts spärlere LLM-Berechnungen aktivieren, und stellten stattdessen fest, dass sie frühe Schichtdarstellungen verdichten, mit schwacher Korrelation zur Ausgabestabilität. Das RIDE-Framework bietet Diagnosen zur Optimierung des Routing-Designs und der Unsicherheitsschätzung in großen Sprachmodellen.

es.title: Route-Induced Density and Stability (RIDE): Intervención controlada y análisis del mecanismo de meta-indicaciones de estilo de enrutamiento en estados internos de LLM
es.summary: Los investigadores cuestionaron la suposición de que las indicaciones de estilo de enrutamiento activan cálculos LLM más dispersos, encontrando en cambio que densifican las representaciones de capas tempranas con una débil correlación con la estabilidad del resultado. El marco RIDE proporciona diagnósticos para optimizar el diseño de enrutamiento y la estimación de incertidumbre en modelos de lenguaje grandes.

title: Xuanwu: Evolving General Multimodal Models into an Industrial-Grade Foundation for Content Ecosystems
summary: Xuanwu VL-2B demonstrates how compact 2B-parameter multimodal models achieve industrial-grade performance for content moderation and adversarial scenarios, outperforming larger competitors like Gemini while maintaining deployment efficiency. The model balances fine-grained visual perception with computational constraints through progressive multi-stage training.
content:
arXiv:2603.29211v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In recent years, multimodal large models have continued to improve on general benchmarks. However, in real-world content moderation and adversarial settings, mainstream models still suffer from degraded generalization and catastrophic forgetting because of limited fine-grained visual perception and insufficient modeling of long-tail noise. In this paper, we present Xuanwu VL-2B as a case study of how general multimodal models can be developed into an industrial-grade foundation model for content ecosystems. The model adopts a compact InternViT-300M + MLP + Qwen3 1.7B architecture, balancing fine-grained visual perception, language-semantic alignment, and deployment cost within an approximately 2B-parameter budget. To balance business specialization with the retention of general capabilities, we developed a data iteration and curation mechanism and trained the model through a progressive three-stage pipeline: pre-training, mid-training, and post-training. Ablation studies and offline business evaluations show that Xuanwu VL-2B achieves an average score of 67.90 across seven OpenCompass multimodal metrics (vs. 64.27 for InternVL 3.5 2B), an average recall of 94.38% over seven independent business moderation tasks, and a weighted overall recall of 82.82% on policy-violating text in challenging adversarial OCR scenarios, outperforming Gemini-2.5-Pro (76.72%). These results show that, under a limited parameter budget, Xuanwu VL-2B achieves a practical balance among business alignment, visual perception, general capability retention, and deployment cost.

zh.title: 璇武：将通用多模态模型发展为内容生态系统的工业级基础
zh.summary: Xuanwu VL-2B展示了紧凑的2B参数多模态模型如何为内容审核和对抗场景实现工业级性能，超越Gemini等更大型竞争对手，同时保持部署效率。该模型通过渐进式多阶段训练在细粒度视觉感知与计算约束之间取得平衡。

fr.title: Xuanwu : Évoluer les modèles multimodaux généraux vers une fondation de niveau industriel pour les écosystèmes de contenu
fr.summary: Xuanwu VL-2B démontre comment les modèles multimodaux compacts de 2B paramètres réalisent des performances de niveau industriel pour la modération de contenu et les scénarios adversariales, surpassant les concurrents plus grands comme Gemini tout en maintenant l'efficacité du déploiement. Le modèle équilibre la perception visuelle fine avec les contraintes de calcul grâce à un entraînement progressif multi-étapes.

de.title: Xuanwu: Weiterentwicklung allgemeiner multimodaler Modelle zu einer industrietauglichen Grundlage für Content-Ökosysteme
de.summary: Xuanwu VL-2B zeigt, wie kompakte 2B-Parameter-Multimodal-Modelle industrietaugliche Leistung für Content-Moderation und gegnerische Szenarien erreichen und dabei größere Konkurrenten wie Gemini übertreffen und gleichzeitig Bereitstellungseffizienz beibehalten. Das Modell balanciert feinkörnige visuelle Wahrnehmung mit Recheneinschränkungen durch progressives mehrstufiges Training.

es.title: Xuanwu: Evolución de modelos multimodales generales hacia una base de nivel industrial para ecosistemas de contenido
es.summary: Xuanwu VL-2B demuestra cómo los modelos multimodales compactos de parámetros 2B logran un rendimiento de nivel industrial para moderación de contenido y escenarios adversariales, superando a competidores más grandes como Gemini mientras mantiene eficiencia de implementación. El modelo equilibra la percepción visual de grano fino con restricciones computacionales mediante entrenamiento progresivo multietapa.

title: Beyond pass@1: A Reliability Science Framework for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
summary: A new reliability framework reveals that LLM capability and reliability diverge significantly for longer-horizon tasks, with frontier models showing meltdown rates up to 19% despite high capability scores. The framework introduces metrics like Reliability Decay Curve to evaluate consistent performance across repeated attempts.
content:
arXiv:2603.29231v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Existing benchmarks measure capability -- whether a model succeeds on a single attempt -- but production deployments
  require reliability -- consistent success across repeated attempts on tasks of varying duration. We show these
  properties diverge systematically as task duration grows, and that pass@1 on short tasks is structurally blind to
  this divergence.
  We introduce a reliability science framework for long-horizon LLM agents with four metrics: Reliability Decay Curve
  (RDC), Variance Amplification Factor (VAF), Graceful Degradation Score (GDS), and Meltdown Onset Point (MOP). We
  evaluate 10 models across 23,392 episodes on a 396-task benchmark spanning four duration buckets and three domains.
  Key findings: (1) reliability decay is domain-stratified -- SE GDS drops from 0.90 to 0.44 while document processing
  is nearly flat (0.74 to 0.71); (2) VAF bifurcates by capability tier -- high VAF is a capability signature, not an
  instability signal; (3) capability and reliability rankings diverge substantially, with multi-rank inversions at long
  horizons; (4) frontier models have the highest meltdown rates (up to 19%) because they attempt ambitious multi-step
  strategies that sometimes spiral; and (5) memory scaffolds universally hurt long-horizon performance across all 10
  models. These results motivate reliability as a first-class evaluation dimension alongside capability.

zh.title: 超越pass@1：长视野LLM智能体的可靠性科学框架
zh.summary: 一个新的可靠性框架显示，LLM能力与可靠性在更长视野任务中显著分化，前沿模型的熔断率高达19%，尽管能力评分很高。该框架引入了可靠性衰减曲线等指标来评估跨重复尝试的一致性能。

fr.title: Au-delà de pass@1 : Un cadre de science de la fiabilité pour les agents LLM à long horizon
fr.summary: Un nouveau cadre de fiabilité révèle que la capacité LLM et la fiabilité divergent considérablement pour les tâches à plus long horizon, les modèles frontière montrant des taux de défaillance jusqu'à 19% malgré des scores de capacité élevés. Le cadre introduit des mesures comme la courbe de déclin de fiabilité pour évaluer les performances cohérentes sur les tentatives répétées.

de.title: Jenseits von pass@1: Ein Zuverlässigkeitswissenschafts-Framework für langfristige LLM-Agenten
de.summary: Ein neues Zuverlässigkeits-Framework offenbart, dass sich LLM-Fähigkeit und Zuverlässigkeit bei längerfristigen Aufgaben erheblich unterscheiden, wobei Spitzenmodelle Ausfallquoten von bis zu 19% aufweisen, obwohl sie hohe Leistungswerte haben. Das Framework führt Metriken wie Zuverlässigkeitsabfallskurve ein, um konsistente Leistung über wiederholte Versuche hinweg zu bewerten.

es.title: Más allá de pass@1: Un marco de ciencia de confiabilidad para agentes LLM de largo horizonte
es.summary: Un nuevo marco de confiabilidad revela que la capacidad y confiabilidad de LLM divergen significativamente para tareas de horizonte más largo, con modelos fronterizos mostrando tasas de falla de hasta 19% a pesar de puntuaciones de capacidad altas. El marco introduce métricas como la curva de decaimiento de confiabilidad para evaluar el rendimiento consistente en intentos repetidos.

title: Grokking From Abstraction to Intelligence
summary: Researchers provide new mechanistic understanding of neural network grokking, showing it results from spontaneous simplification and compression of internal structures through manifold collapse. Causal and spectral analysis reveals how models transition from memorization to generalization at a fundamental level.
content:
arXiv:2603.29262v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Grokking in modular arithmetic has established itself as the quintessential fruit fly experiment, serving as a critical domain for investigating the mechanistic origins of model generalization. Despite its significance, existing research remains narrowly focused on specific local circuits or optimization tuning, largely overlooking the global structural evolution that fundamentally drives this phenomenon. We propose that grokking originates from a spontaneous simplification of internal model structures governed by the principle of parsimony. We integrate causal, spectral, and algorithmic complexity measures alongside Singular Learning Theory to reveal that the transition from memorization to generalization corresponds to the physical collapse of redundant manifolds and deep information compression, offering a novel perspective for understanding the mechanisms of model overfitting and generalization.

zh.title: 顿悟：从抽象到智能
zh.summary: 研究人员提供了神经网络顿悟的新机制理解，表明它源于内部结构的自发简化和压缩，通过流形崩塌。因果分析和谱分析揭示了模型如何在根本层面从记忆化过渡到泛化。

fr.title: Grokking : De l'abstraction à l'intelligence
fr.summary: Les chercheurs fournissent une nouvelle compréhension mécaniste du grokking des réseaux de neurones, montrant qu'il résulte de la simplification spontanée et de la compression des structures internes par l'effondrement de variété. L'analyse causale et spectrale révèle comment les modèles transitent de la mémorisation à la généralisation à un niveau fondamental.

de.title: Grokking: Von Abstraktion zu Intelligenz
de.summary: Forscher bieten ein neues mechanistisches Verständnis des Grokking von neuronalen Netzen und zeigen, dass es aus spontaner Vereinfachung und Kompression innerer Strukturen durch Mannigfaltigkeitskollaps resultiert. Kausale und Spektralanalyse zeigen, wie Modelle auf fundamentaler Ebene vom Memorieren zur Verallgemeinerung übergehen.

es.title: Grokking: De la abstracción a la inteligencia
es.summary: Los investigadores proporcionan una nueva comprensión mecanista del grokking de redes neuronales, mostrando que resulta de la simplificación espontánea y compresión de estructuras internas a través del colapso de variedades. El análisis causal y espectral revela cómo los modelos hacen la transición de la memorización a la generalización a nivel fundamental.

title: PSPA-Bench: A Personalized Benchmark for Smartphone GUI Agent
summary: PSPA-Bench introduces the first benchmark for evaluating smartphone GUI agent personalization, containing over 12,000 user-specific instructions across real-world mobile scenarios. Results show current agents struggle with personalized adaptation and highlight the importance of reasoning, perception, and memory mechanisms.
content:
arXiv:2603.29318v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Smartphone GUI agents execute tasks by operating directly on app interfaces, offering a path to broad capability without deep system integration. However, real-world smartphone use is highly personalized: users adopt diverse workflows and preferences, challenging agents to deliver customized assistance rather than generic solutions. Existing GUI agent benchmarks cannot adequately capture this personalization dimension due to sparse user-specific data and the lack of fine-grained evaluation metrics. To address this gap, we present PSPA-Bench, the benchmark dedicated to evaluating personalization in smartphone GUI agents. PSPA-Bench comprises over 12,855 personalized instructions aligned with real-world user behaviors across 10 representative daily-use scenarios and 22 mobile apps, and introduces a structure-aware process evaluation method that measures agents' personalized capabilities at a fine-grained level. Through PSPA-Bench, we benchmark 11 state-of-the-art GUI agents. Results reveal that current methods perform poorly under personalized settings, with even the strongest agent achieving limited success. Our analysis further highlights three directions for advancing personalized GUI agents: (1) reasoning-oriented models consistently outperform general LLMs, (2) perception remains a simple yet critical capability, and (3) reflection and long-term memory mechanisms are key to improving adaptation. Together, these findings establish PSPA-Bench as a foundation for systematic study and future progress in personalized GUI agents.

zh.title: PSPA-Bench: 智能手机GUI代理个性化基准
zh.summary: PSPA-Bench推出了首个评估智能手机GUI代理个性化的基准，包含超过12,000条用户特定指令，涵盖真实移动场景。结果表明当前代理在个性化适应方面存在困难，突出了推理、感知和记忆机制的重要性。

fr.title: PSPA-Bench: Un benchmark personnalisé pour les agents GUI mobiles
fr.summary: PSPA-Bench introduit le premier benchmark pour évaluer la personnalisation des agents GUI mobiles, contenant plus de 12 000 instructions spécifiques à l'utilisateur dans des scénarios mobiles réels. Les résultats montrent que les agents actuels ont du mal à s'adapter à la personnalisation et mettent en évidence l'importance des mécanismes de raisonnement, de perception et de mémoire.

de.title: PSPA-Bench: Ein personalisiertes Benchmark für Smartphone-GUI-Agenten
de.summary: PSPA-Bench führt das erste Benchmark zur Bewertung der Personalisierung von Smartphone-GUI-Agenten ein, das über 12.000 benutzerspezifische Anweisungen in realen mobilen Szenarien enthält. Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass aktuelle Agenten Schwierigkeiten bei der personalisierten Anpassung haben, und unterstreichen die Bedeutung von Reasoning-, Wahrnehmungs- und Gedächtnismechanismen.

es.title: PSPA-Bench: Un benchmark personalizado para agentes GUI de smartphones
es.summary: PSPA-Bench presenta el primer benchmark para evaluar la personalización de agentes GUI en teléfonos inteligentes, conteniendo más de 12,000 instrucciones específicas del usuario en escenarios móviles del mundo real. Los resultados muestran que los agentes actuales tienen dificultades con la adaptación personalizada y destacan la importancia de los mecanismos de razonamiento, percepción y memoria.

title: Nomad: Autonomous Exploration and Discovery
summary: Nomad is a system for autonomous data exploration that discovers insights without pre-defined queries, using an Exploration Map and verifier agents to systematically balance breadth and depth. The system produces trustworthy, cited reports and demonstrates superior quality compared to query-driven baselines.
content:
arXiv:2603.29353v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We introduce Nomad, a system for autonomous data exploration and insight discovery. Given a corpus of documents, databases, or other data sources, users rarely know the full set of questions, hypotheses, or connections that could be explored. As a result, query-driven question answering and prompt-driven deep-research systems remain limited by human framing and often fail to cover the broader insight space.
  Nomad addresses this problem with an exploration-first architecture. It constructs an explicit Exploration Map over the domain and systematically traverses it to balance breadth and depth. It generates and selects hypotheses and investigates them with an explorer agent that can use document search, web search, and database tools. Candidate insights are then checked by an independent verifier before entering a reporting pipeline that produces cited reports and higher-level meta-reports.
  We also present a comprehensive evaluation framework for autonomous discovery systems that measures trustworthiness, report quality, and diversity. Using a corpus of selected UN and WHO reports, we show that \nomad{} produces more trustworthy and higher-quality reports than baselines, while also producing more diverse insights over several runs.
  Nomad is a step toward autonomous systems that not only answer user questions or conduct directed research, but also discover which questions, research directions, and insights are worth surfacing in the first place.

zh.title: Nomad: 自主探索与发现
zh.summary: Nomad是一个自主数据探索系统，通过使用探索地图和验证器代理来发现见解而无需预定义查询，系统地平衡广度和深度。该系统生成可信的、有引用的报告，与基于查询的基线相比表现出更优质的结果。

fr.title: Nomad: Exploration autonome et découverte
fr.summary: Nomad est un système d'exploration de données autonome qui découvre des insights sans requêtes prédéfinies, utilisant une Carte d'Exploration et des agents vérificateurs pour équilibrer systématiquement la portée et la profondeur. Le système produit des rapports fiables et cités, et démontre une qualité supérieure par rapport aux baselines pilotés par les requêtes.

de.title: Nomad: Autonome Erkundung und Entdeckung
de.summary: Nomad ist ein System zur autonomen Datenerkundung, das Einblicke ohne vordefinierte Abfragen entdeckt und eine Explorationskarte sowie Verifieragenten verwendet, um systematisch Breite und Tiefe auszugleichen. Das System erzeugt vertrauenswürdige, zitierte Berichte und zeigt überlegene Qualität im Vergleich zu abfragengesteuerten Baselines.

es.title: Nomad: Exploración autónoma y descubrimiento
es.summary: Nomad es un sistema para la exploración de datos autónoma que descubre información sin consultas predefinidas, utilizando un Mapa de Exploración y agentes verificadores para equilibrar sistemáticamente amplitud y profundidad. El sistema produce informes confiables y citados, y demuestra una calidad superior en comparación con líneas base impulsadas por consultas.

title: BenchScope: How Many Independent Signals Does Your Benchmark Provide?
summary: BenchScope introduces Effective Dimensionality (ED) to measure redundancy in AI benchmarks, revealing that popular benchmarks contain substantial duplicate information with far fewer effective measurement axes than published scores. The diagnostic tool helps maintainers identify and remove redundant benchmark components.
content:
arXiv:2603.29357v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: AI evaluation suites often report many scores without checking whether those scores carry independent information. We introduce Effective Dimensionality (ED), the participation ratio of a centered benchmark-score spectrum, as a fast, population-conditional upper-bound diagnostic of measurement breadth.
