Applied at per-instance granularity to 22 benchmarks across 8 domains and more than 8,400 model evaluations, ED reveals substantial redundancy: the six-score Open LLM Leaderboard behaves like roughly two effective measurement axes (ED = 1.7), BBH and MMLU-Pro are near-interchangeable (rho = 0.96, stable across seven subpopulations), and measurement breadth varies more than 20x across current benchmarks. We show that relative ED rankings are stable under matched-dimension controls and that ED can flag redundant suite components, monitor performance-conditional compression, and guide benchmark maintenance. Because binary spectra overestimate absolute latent dimensionality, we interpret ED as a screening statistic rather than a literal factor count and complement it with null, reliability, and saturation analyses. We provide a 22-benchmark reference atlas and a four-step diagnostic workflow that benchmark maintainers can run with a score matrix and a few lines of code.

zh.title: BenchScope: 您的基准提供多少个独立信号？
zh.summary: BenchScope引入有效维度（ED）来测量AI基准中的冗余性，揭示流行的基准包含大量重复信息，有效测量轴远少于发布的分数。该诊断工具帮助维护人员识别并删除冗余的基准组件。

fr.title: BenchScope: Combien de signaux indépendants votre benchmark fournit-il?
fr.summary: BenchScope introduit la Dimensionnalité Effective (DE) pour mesurer la redondance dans les benchmarks d'IA, révélant que les benchmarks populaires contiennent des informations dupliquées substantielles avec beaucoup moins d'axes de mesure efficaces que les scores publiés. L'outil de diagnostic aide les mainteneurs à identifier et supprimer les composants de benchmark redondants.

de.title: BenchScope: Wie viele unabhängige Signale liefert dein Benchmark?
de.summary: BenchScope führt die Effektive Dimensionalität (ED) ein, um Redundanz in KI-Benchmarks zu messen, und offenbart, dass beliebte Benchmarks erhebliche duplizierte Informationen mit weitaus weniger effektiven Mesachsen als veröffentlichte Scores enthalten. Das Diagnose-Tool hilft Betreuern, redundante Benchmark-Komponenten zu identifizieren und zu entfernen.

es.title: BenchScope: ¿Cuántas señales independientes proporciona tu benchmark?
es.summary: BenchScope introduce la Dimensionalidad Efectiva (ED) para medir la redundancia en puntos de referencia de IA, revelando que los puntos de referencia populares contienen información duplicada sustancial con muchos menos ejes de medición efectivos que las puntuaciones publicadas. La herramienta de diagnóstico ayuda a los mantenedores a identificar y eliminar componentes de referencia redundantes.

title: Rigorous Explanations for Tree Ensembles
summary: This work develops logically-sound, rigorous explanation methods for tree ensemble models like random forests and boosted trees to build interpretability and trust. The approach ensures explanations truly reflect underlying predictor behavior rather than providing post-hoc approximations.
content:
arXiv:2603.29361v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Tree ensembles (TEs) find a multitude of practical applications. They represent one of the most general and accurate classes of machine learning methods. While they are typically quite concise in representation, their operation remains inscrutable to human decision makers. One solution to build trust in the operation of TEs is to automatically identify explanations for the predictions made. Evidently, we can only achieve trust using explanations, if those explanations are rigorous, that is truly reflect properties of the underlying predictor they explain This paper investigates the computation of rigorously-defined, logically-sound explanations for the concrete case of two well-known examples of tree ensembles, namely random forests and boosted trees.

zh.title: 树集合的严谨解释
zh.summary: 这项工作为树集合模型（如随机森林和梯度提升树）开发了逻辑严密、严谨的解释方法，以建立可解释性和信任。该方法确保解释真实反映底层预测器的行为，而不是提供事后近似。

fr.title: Explications rigoureuses pour les ensembles d'arbres
fr.summary: Ce travail développe des méthodes d'explication logiquement solides et rigoureuses pour les modèles d'ensembles d'arbres comme les forêts aléatoires et les arbres boostés afin de construire l'interprétabilité et la confiance. L'approche garantit que les explications reflètent vraiment le comportement du prédicteur sous-jacent plutôt que de fournir des approximations post-hoc.

de.title: Strenge Erklärungen für Baumensembles
de.summary: Diese Arbeit entwickelt logisch fundierte, strenge Erklärungsmethoden für Baumensemble-Modelle wie Random Forests und Boosted Trees, um Interpretierbarkeit und Vertrauen aufzubauen. Der Ansatz stellt sicher, dass Erklärungen das tatsächliche Verhalten des zugrunde liegenden Prädiktors widerspiegeln, anstatt Post-hoc-Approximationen bereitzustellen.

es.title: Explicaciones rigurosas para conjuntos de árboles
es.summary: Este trabajo desarrolla métodos de explicación lógicamente sólidos y rigurosos para modelos de conjuntos de árboles como bosques aleatorios y árboles potenciados para construir interpretabilidad y confianza. El enfoque garantiza que las explicaciones reflejen verdaderamente el comportamiento del predictor subyacente en lugar de proporcionar aproximaciones post-hoc.

title: AI-Generated Prior Authorization Letters: Strong Clinical Content, Weak Administrative Scaffolding
summary: Three major LLMs generated clinically sound prior authorization letters but consistently lacked critical administrative requirements like billing codes and authorization duration. The findings show that clinical capability alone isn't sufficient; healthcare deployment requires systems providing administrative precision alongside clinical accuracy.
content:
arXiv:2603.29366v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Prior authorization remains one of the most burdensome administrative processes in U.S. healthcare, consuming billions of dollars and thousands of physician hours each year. While large language models have shown promise across clinical text tasks, their ability to produce submission-ready prior authorization letters has received only limited attention, with existing work confined to single-case demonstrations rather than structured multi-scenario evaluation. We assessed three commercially available LLMs (GPT-4o, Claude Sonnet 4.5, and Gemini 2.5 Pro) across 45 physician-validated synthetic scenarios spanning rheumatology, psychiatry, oncology, cardiology, and orthopedics. All three models generated letters with strong clinical content: accurate diagnoses, well-structured medical necessity arguments, and thorough step therapy documentation. However, a secondary analysis of real-world administrative requirements revealed consistent gaps that clinical scoring alone did not capture, including absent billing codes, missing authorization duration requests, and inadequate follow-up plans. These findings reframe the question: the challenge for clinical deployment is not whether LLMs can write clinically adequate letters, but whether the systems built around them can supply the administrative precision that payer workflows require.

zh.title: AI生成的事前授权信函：强大的临床内容，薄弱的行政框架
zh.summary: 三个主要的大型语言模型生成了临床上合理的事前授权信函，但始终缺乏关键的行政要求，如计费代码和授权期限。研究结果表明，仅有临床能力是不够的；医疗保健部署需要系统同时提供行政精确性和临床准确性。

fr.title: Lettres d'autorisation préalable générées par l'IA : contenu clinique solide, échafaudage administratif faible
fr.summary: Trois grands modèles de langage ont généré des lettres d'autorisation préalable cliniquement valides mais ont systématiquement manqué d'exigences administratives essentielles telles que les codes de facturation et la durée d'autorisation. Les résultats montrent que la seule capacité clinique ne suffit pas ; le déploiement dans le secteur de la santé nécessite des systèmes fournissant la précision administrative à côté de la précision clinique.

de.title: Von KI generierte Prior-Authorization-Schreiben: Starker klinischer Inhalt, schwache administrative Struktur
de.summary: Drei große Sprachmodelle generierten klinisch fundierte Prior-Authorization-Schreiben, denen aber konsequent kritische administrative Anforderungen wie Abrechnungscodes und Autorisierungsdauer fehlten. Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass klinische Fähigkeiten allein nicht ausreichen; die Bereitstellung im Gesundheitswesen erfordert Systeme, die administrative Genauigkeit neben klinischer Genauigkeit bieten.

es.title: Cartas de autorización previa generadas por IA: contenido clínico fuerte, andamiaje administrativo débil
es.summary: Tres grandes modelos de lenguaje generaron cartas de autorización previa clínicamente sólidas pero carecían sistemáticamente de requisitos administrativos críticos como códigos de facturación y duración de la autorización. Los hallazgos muestran que la capacidad clínica por sí sola no es suficiente; el despliegue en salud requiere sistemas que proporcionen precisión administrativa junto con precisión clínica.

title: ELT-Bench-Verified: Benchmark Quality Issues Underestimate AI Agent Capabilities
summary: Researchers audited ELT-Bench and found benchmark quality issues—rigid evaluation scripts, ambiguous specifications, and incorrect ground truth—significantly underestimated AI agent capabilities for data pipeline construction. Creating ELT-Bench-Verified with corrected benchmarks revealed substantial capability improvements.
content:
arXiv:2603.29399v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Constructing Extract-Load-Transform (ELT) pipelines is a labor-intensive data engineering task and a high-impact target for AI automation. On ELT-Bench, the first benchmark for end-to-end ELT pipeline construction, AI agents initially showed low success rates, suggesting they lacked practical utility.
  We revisit these results and identify two factors causing a substantial underestimation of agent capabilities. First, re-evaluating ELT-Bench with upgraded large language models reveals that the extraction and loading stage is largely solved, while transformation performance improves significantly. Second, we develop an Auditor-Corrector methodology that combines scalable LLM-driven root-cause analysis with rigorous human validation (inter-annotator agreement Fleiss' kappa = 0.85) to audit benchmark quality. Applying this to ELT-Bench uncovers that most failed transformation tasks contain benchmark-attributable errors -- including rigid evaluation scripts, ambiguous specifications, and incorrect ground truth -- that penalize correct agent outputs.
  Based on these findings, we construct ELT-Bench-Verified, a revised benchmark with refined evaluation logic and corrected ground truth. Re-evaluating on this version yields significant improvement attributable entirely to benchmark correction. Our results show that both rapid model improvement and benchmark quality issues contributed to underestimating agent capabilities. More broadly, our findings echo observations of pervasive annotation errors in text-to-SQL benchmarks, suggesting quality issues are systemic in data engineering evaluation. Systematic quality auditing should be standard practice for complex agentic tasks. We release ELT-Bench-Verified to provide a more reliable foundation for progress in AI-driven data engineering automation.

zh.title: ELT-Bench-Verified：基准质量问题低估AI代理能力
zh.summary: 研究人员审计了ELT-Bench，发现基准质量问题——刚性评估脚本、模糊规范和不正确的基准真实值——显著低估了AI代理在数据管道构建中的能力。创建具有更正基准的ELT-Bench-Verified揭示了能力的重大改进。

fr.title: ELT-Bench-Verified : Les problèmes de qualité des benchmarks sous-estiment les capacités des agents IA
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont audité ELT-Bench et ont trouvé des problèmes de qualité de benchmark - des scripts d'évaluation rigides, des spécifications ambigües et une vérité de base incorrecte - qui sous-estimaient considérablement les capacités des agents IA pour la construction de pipelines de données. La création d'ELT-Bench-Verified avec des benchmarks corrigés a révélé des améliorations substantielles des capacités.

de.title: ELT-Bench-Verified: Qualitätsprobleme bei Benchmarks unterschätzen KI-Agent-Fähigkeiten
de.summary: Forscher führten eine Prüfung von ELT-Bench durch und fanden Qualitätsprobleme bei Benchmarks - starre Evaluierungsskripte, mehrdeutige Spezifikationen und falsche Ground Truth - die die Fähigkeiten von KI-Agenten bei der Datenpipelinekonstruktion erheblich unterschätzten. Die Erstellung von ELT-Bench-Verified mit korrigierten Benchmarks offenbarte erhebliche Verbesserungen der Fähigkeiten.

es.title: ELT-Bench-Verified: Los problemas de calidad del benchmark subestiman las capacidades del agente de IA
es.summary: Los investigadores auditaron ELT-Bench y encontraron problemas de calidad del benchmark (scripts de evaluación rígidos, especificaciones ambiguas y verdad fundamental incorrecta) que subestimaban significativamente las capacidades del agente de IA para la construcción de canalizaciones de datos. La creación de ELT-Bench-Verified con benchmarks corregidos reveló mejoras sustanciales en las capacidades.

title: Structural Compactness as a Complementary Criterion for Explanation Quality
summary: Researchers introduce Minimum Spanning Tree Compactness (MST-C), a geometric metric for evaluating how spatially cohesive and organized feature attributions are in neural network explanations. MST-C complements existing metrics by capturing structural properties that influence explanation legibility.
content:
arXiv:2603.29491v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In the evaluation of attribution quality, the quantitative assessment of explanation legibility is particularly difficult, as it is influenced by varying shapes and internal organization of attributions not captured by simple statistics. To address this issue, we introduce Minimum Spanning Tree Compactness (MST-C), a graph-based structural metric that captures higher-order geometric properties of attributions, such as spread and cohesion. These components are combined into a single score that evaluates compactness, favoring attributions with salient points spread across a small area and spatially organized into few but cohesive clusters. We show that MST-C reliably distinguishes between explanation methods, exposes fundamental structural differences between models, and provides a robust, self-contained diagnostic for explanation compactness that complements existing notions of attribution complexity.

zh.title: 结构紧凑性作为解释质量的补充标准
zh.summary: 研究人员引入了最小生成树紧凑性（MST-C），这是一个几何指标，用于评估神经网络解释中特征归因的空间内聚性和组织性。MST-C通过捕捉影响解释清晰度的结构属性，补充了现有指标。

fr.title: La compacité structurelle comme critère complémentaire pour la qualité de l'explication
fr.summary: Les chercheurs introduisent la compacité du minimum de l'arbre couvrant (MST-C), une métrique géométrique pour évaluer la cohésion spatiale et l'organisation des attributions de caractéristiques dans les explications de réseaux de neurones. MST-C complète les métriques existantes en capturant les propriétés structurelles qui influencent la lisibilité des explications.

de.title: Strukturelle Kompaktheit als ergänzendes Kriterium für Erklärungsqualität
de.summary: Forscher führen die Kompaktheit des minimalen Spannbaums (MST-C) ein, eine geometrische Metrik zur Bewertung der räumlichen Kohäsion und Organisation von Merkmalszuschreibungen in Erklärungen neuronaler Netze. MST-C ergänzt bestehende Metriken durch die Erfassung struktureller Eigenschaften, die die Lesbarkeit von Erklärungen beeinflussen.

es.title: Compacidad estructural como criterio complementario para la calidad de la explicación
es.summary: Los investigadores introducen la Compacidad del Árbol de Expansión Mínima (MST-C), una métrica geométrica para evaluar la cohesión espacial y la organización de las atribuciones de características en explicaciones de redes neuronales. MST-C complementa las métricas existentes al capturar propiedades estructurales que influyen en la legibilidad de las explicaciones.

title: Metriplector: From Field Theory to Neural Architecture
summary: Metriplector is a neural architecture primitive grounded in field theory where inputs configure physical systems and computation emerges from their dynamics, achieving perfect pathfinding and Sudoku performance while scaling to competitive image and language modeling results. The unified framework demonstrates how physics-inspired computation generalizes across diverse domains.
content:
arXiv:2603.29496v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present Metriplector, a neural architecture primitive in which the input configures an abstract physical system--fields, sources, and operators--and the dynamics of that system is the computation. Multiple fields evolve via coupled metriplectic dynamics, and the stress-energy tensor T^{{\mu}{\nu}}, derived from Noether's theorem, provides the readout. The metriplectic formulation admits a natural spectrum of instantiations: the dissipative branch alone yields a screened Poisson equation solved exactly via conjugate gradient; activating the full structure--including the antisymmetric Poisson bracket--gives field dynamics for image recognition and language modeling. We evaluate Metriplector across four domains, each using a task-specific architecture built from this shared primitive with progressively richer physics: F1=1.0 on maze pathfinding, generalizing from 15x15 training grids to unseen 39x39 grids; 97.2% exact Sudoku solve rate with zero structural injection; 81.03% on CIFAR-100 with 2.26M parameters; and 1.182 bits/byte on language modeling with 3.6x fewer training tokens than a GPT baseline.

zh.title: Metriplector：从场论到神经架构
zh.summary: Metriplector是一个以场论为基础的神经架构原语，其中输入配置物理系统，计算从它们的动力学中出现，在完美寻路和数独性能的同时扩展到竞争性图像和语言建模结果。统一框架展示了物理启发的计算如何在不同领域中泛化。

fr.title: Metriplector : De la théorie des champs à l'architecture neuronale
fr.summary: Metriplector est une primitive d'architecture neuronale enracinée dans la théorie des champs où les entrées configurent des systèmes physiques et le calcul émerge de leurs dynamiques, réalisant une performance parfaite en pathfinding et Sudoku tout en s'étendant à des résultats compétitifs en modélisation d'images et de langage. Le cadre unifié démontre comment le calcul inspiré par la physique se généralise dans des domaines divers.

de.title: Metriplector: Von der Feldtheorie zur neuronalen Architektur
de.summary: Metriplector ist eine in der Feldtheorie verankerte neuronale Architektur-Primitive, in der Eingaben physikalische Systeme konfigurieren und Berechnung aus ihrer Dynamik entsteht, perfekte Pfadfindungs- und Sudoku-Leistung erzielt und gleichzeitig auf konkurrenzierende Ergebnisse bei Bild- und Sprachmodellierung skaliert. Das einheitliche Framework demonstriert, wie physik-inspirierte Berechnung über verschiedene Domänen verallgemeinert.

es.title: Metriplector: De la teoría de campos a la arquitectura neuronal
es.summary: Metriplector es una primitiva de arquitectura neuronal fundamentada en la teoría de campos donde las entradas configuran sistemas físicos y el cálculo surge de su dinámica, logrando un rendimiento perfecto en búsqueda de caminos y Sudoku mientras se escala a resultados competitivos en modelado de imágenes y lenguaje. El marco unificado demuestra cómo la computación inspirada en la física se generaliza en dominios diversos.

title: Learning to Generate Formally Verifiable Step-by-Step Logic Reasoning via Structured Formal Intermediaries
summary: PRoSFI enhances LLM reasoning reliability by generating structured intermediate steps verified by formal provers rather than relying on outcome rewards that mask flawed reasoning. This process-based approach produces more credible multi-step reasoning by ensuring each step is machine-checkable.
content:
arXiv:2603.29500v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have recently demonstrated impressive performance on complex, multi-step reasoning tasks, especially when post-trained with outcome-rewarded reinforcement learning Guo et al. 2025. However, it has been observed that outcome rewards often overlook flawed intermediate steps, leading to unreliable reasoning steps even when final answers are correct. To address this unreliable reasoning, we propose PRoSFI (Process Reward over Structured Formal Intermediates), a novel reward method that enhances reasoning reliability without compromising accuracy. Instead of generating formal proofs directly, which is rarely accomplishable for a modest-sized (7B) model, the model outputs structured intermediate steps aligned with its natural language reasoning. Each step is then verified by a formal prover. Only fully validated reasoning chains receive high rewards. The integration of formal verification guides the model towards generating step-by-step machine-checkable proofs, thereby yielding more credible final answers. PRoSFI offers a simple and effective approach to training trustworthy reasoning models.

zh.title: 学习通过结构化形式中间体生成可形式验证的逐步逻辑推理
zh.summary: PRoSFI通过生成由形式证明器验证的结构化中间步骤来增强LLM推理的可靠性，而不是依赖于掩盖有缺陷推理的结果奖励。这种基于过程的方法通过确保每一步都可以被机器检查，产生更可信的多步推理。

fr.title: Apprendre à générer un raisonnement logique étape par étape formellement vérifiable via des intermédiaires formels structurés
fr.summary: PRoSFI améliore la fiabilité du raisonnement des LLM en générant des étapes intermédiaires structurées vérifiées par des prouveurs formels plutôt que de compter sur des récompenses de résultat qui masquent le raisonnement défectueux. Cette approche basée sur le processus produit un raisonnement multi-étapes plus crédible en garantissant que chaque étape est vérifiable par machine.

de.title: Erlernen der Erzeugung von formal verifizierbarem schrittweisem logischem Denken über strukturierte formale Zwischenstufen
de.summary: PRoSFI verbessert die Zuverlässigkeit des LLM-Denkens durch die Erzeugung strukturierter Zwischenschritte, die von formalen Beweisen überprüft werden, anstatt sich auf Ergebnisvergütungen zu verlassen, die fehlerhaftes Denken maskieren. Dieser prozessbasierte Ansatz erzeugt glaubwürdigeres mehrstufiges Denken, indem sichergestellt wird, dass jeder Schritt maschinenüberprüfbar ist.

es.title: Aprender a generar razonamiento lógico paso a paso formalmente verificable a través de intermediarios formales estructurados
es.summary: PRoSFI mejora la confiabilidad del razonamiento del LLM al generar pasos intermedios estructurados verificados por provadores formales en lugar de depender de recompensas de resultados que ocultan razonamiento defectuoso. Este enfoque basado en procesos produce un razonamiento multi-paso más creíble al garantizar que cada paso sea verificable por máquina.

title: FlowPIE: Test-Time Scientific Idea Evolution with Flow-Guided Literature Exploration
summary: FlowPIE is a retrieval-generation framework that co-evolves literature exploration and idea generation to improve scientific idea generation for autonomous research. Using flow-guided Monte Carlo Tree Search and LLM-based reward models, it generates diverse, high-quality research ideas while reducing information bias.
content:
arXiv:2603.29557v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Scientific idea generation (SIG) is critical to AI-driven autonomous research, yet existing approaches are often constrained by a static retrieval-then-generation paradigm, leading to homogeneous and insufficiently divergent ideas. In this work, we propose FlowPIE, a tightly coupled retrieval-generation framework that treats literature exploration and idea generation as a co-evolving process. FlowPIE expands literature trajectories via a flow-guided Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) inspired by GFlowNets, using the quality of current ideas assessed by an LLM-based generative reward model (GRM) as a supervised signal to guide adaptive retrieval and construct a diverse, high-quality initial population. Based on this population, FlowPIE models idea generation as a test-time idea evolution process, applying selection, crossover, and mutation with the isolation island paradigm and GRM-based fitness computation to incorporate cross-domain knowledge. It effectively mitigates the information cocoons arising from over-reliance on parametric knowledge and static literature. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that FlowPIE consistently produces ideas with higher novelty, feasibility and diversity compared to strong LLM-based and agent-based frameworks, while enabling reward scaling during test time.

zh.title: FlowPIE：通过流引导文献探索的测试时刻科学想法演化
zh.summary: FlowPIE是一个检索-生成框架，协同发展文献探索和想法生成以改进自主研究的科学想法生成。使用流引导蒙特卡洛树搜索和基于LLM的奖励模型，它生成多样化、高质量的研究想法，同时减少信息偏差。

fr.title: FlowPIE : Évolution d'idées scientifiques au moment des tests avec exploration littéraire guidée par flux
fr.summary: FlowPIE est un cadre de récupération-génération qui co-évolue l'exploration littéraire et la génération d'idées pour améliorer la génération d'idées scientifiques pour la recherche autonome. En utilisant la recherche d'arbre de Monte-Carlo guidée par flux et les modèles de récompense basés sur LLM, il génère des idées de recherche diversifiées et de haute qualité tout en réduisant le biais informatif.

de.title: FlowPIE: Wissenschaftliche Ideenentwicklung zur Testzeit mit durchflussgesteuerter Literaturerkundung
de.summary: FlowPIE ist ein Abruf-Generierungs-Framework, das die Literaturerkundung und Ideengenerierung koevolutioniert, um die wissenschaftliche Ideengenerierung für autonome Forschung zu verbessern. Unter Verwendung von durchflussgesteuerter Monte-Carlo-Baumsuche und LLM-basierten Reward-Modellen generiert es vielfältige, hochwertige Forschungsideen und reduziert gleichzeitig Informationsverzerrungen.

es.title: FlowPIE: Evolución de ideas científicas en tiempo de prueba con exploración de literatura guiada por flujo
es.summary: FlowPIE es un marco de recuperación-generación que coevoluciona la exploración de literatura y la generación de ideas para mejorar la generación de ideas científicas para investigación autónoma. Utilizando búsqueda de árbol de Monte Carlo guiada por flujo y modelos de recompensa basados en LLM, genera ideas de investigación diversas y de alta calidad mientras reduce el sesgo de información.

title: ASI-Evolve: AI Accelerates AI
summary: ASI-Evolve is an agentic framework that uses AI to accelerate AI development through iterative learn-design-experiment-analyze cycles, automating discovery across data, architectures, and learning algorithms. It demonstrated state-of-the-art results including novel neural architectures, optimized pretraining pipelines, and improved reinforcement learning algorithms.
content:
arXiv:2603.29640v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Can AI accelerate the development of AI itself? While recent agentic systems have shown strong performance on well-scoped tasks with rapid feedback, it remains unclear whether they can tackle the costly, long-horizon, and weakly supervised research loops that drive real AI progress. We present ASI-Evolve, an agentic framework for AI-for-AI research that closes this loop through a learn-design-experiment-analyze cycle. ASI-Evolve augments standard evolutionary agents with two key components: a cognition base that injects accumulated human priors into each round of exploration, and a dedicated analyzer that distills complex experimental outcomes into reusable insights for future iterations. To our knowledge, ASI-Evolve is the first unified framework to demonstrate AI-driven discovery across three central components of AI development: data, architectures, and learning algorithms. In neural architecture design, it discovered 105 SOTA linear attention architectures, with the best discovered model surpassing DeltaNet by +0.97 points, nearly 3x the gain of recent human-designed improvements. In pretraining data curation, the evolved pipeline improves average benchmark performance by +3.96 points, with gains exceeding 18 points on MMLU. In reinforcement learning algorithm design, discovered algorithms outperform GRPO by up to +12.5 points on AMC32, +11.67 points on AIME24, and +5.04 points on OlympiadBench. We further provide initial evidence that this AI-for-AI paradigm can transfer beyond the AI stack through experiments in mathematics and biomedicine. Together, these results suggest that ASI-Evolve represents a promising step toward enabling AI to accelerate AI across the foundational stages of development, offering early evidence for the feasibility of closed-loop AI research.

zh.title: ASI-Evolve：AI加速AI
zh.summary: ASI-Evolve是一个智能体框架，通过迭代的学习-设计-实验-分析循环使用AI来加速AI开发，在数据、架构和学习算法中自动化发现。它展示了最先进的结果，包括新颖的神经架构、优化的预训练管道和改进的强化学习算法。

fr.title: ASI-Evolve : L'IA accélère l'IA
fr.summary: ASI-Evolve est un cadre agentique qui utilise l'IA pour accélérer le développement de l'IA grâce à des cycles itératifs d'apprentissage-conception-expérience-analyse, automatisant la découverte dans les données, les architectures et les algorithmes d'apprentissage. Il a démontré des résultats de pointe, notamment des architectures de réseaux de neurones novelles, des pipelines de préentraînement optimisés et des algorithmes d'apprentissage par renforcement améliorés.

de.title: ASI-Evolve: KI beschleunigt KI
de.summary: ASI-Evolve ist ein agentenbasiertes Framework, das KI nutzt, um die KI-Entwicklung durch iterative Zyklen von Lernen-Designen-Experimentieren-Analysieren zu beschleunigen und Entdeckungen über Daten, Architekturen und Lernalgorithmen zu automatisieren. Es zeigte hochmoderne Ergebnisse, darunter neuartige neuronale Architekturen, optimierte Vortrainingspipelines und verbesserte Verstärkungslernalgorithmen.

es.title: ASI-Evolve: La IA acelera la IA
es.summary: ASI-Evolve es un marco agentivo que utiliza IA para acelerar el desarrollo de IA a través de ciclos iterativos de aprender-diseñar-experimentar-analizar, automatizando el descubrimiento en datos, arquitecturas y algoritmos de aprendizaje. Demostró resultados de última generación, incluidas arquitecturas de redes neuronales novedosas, canalizaciones de preentrenamiento optimizadas y algoritmos de aprendizaje por refuerzo mejorados.

title: Optimizing Donor Outreach for Blood Collection Sessions: A Scalable Decision Support Framework
summary: This paper addresses blood donor recruitment optimization across multi-site networks while balancing eligibility, demand targets, and donor convenience to prevent fatigue. The framework uses binary integer linear programming and greedy heuristics to achieve effective supply-demand matching at scale.
content:
arXiv:2603.29643v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Blood donation centers face challenges in matching supply with demand while managing donor availability. Although targeted outreach is important, it can cause donor fatigue via over-solicitation. Effective recruitment requires targeting the right donors at the right time, balancing constraints with donor convenience and eligibility. Despite extensive work on blood supply chain optimization and growing interest in algorithmic donor recruitment, the operational problem of assigning donors to sessions across a multi-site network, taking into account eligibility, capacity, blood-type demand targets, geographic convenience, and donor safety, remains unaddressed.
  We address this gap with an optimization framework for donor invitation scheduling incorporating donor eligibility, travel convenience, blood-type demand targets, and penalties. We evaluate two strategies: (i) a binary integer linear programming (BILP) formulation and (ii) an efficient greedy heuristic. Evaluation uses the registry from Instituto Portugu\^es do Sangue e da Transplanta\c{c}\~ao (IPST) for invite planning in the Lisbon operational region using 4-month windows. A prospective pipeline integrates organic attendance forecasting, quantile-based demand targets, and residual capacity estimation for forward-looking invitation plans. Results reveal its key role in closing the supply-demand gap in the Lisbon operational region. A controlled comparison shows that the greedy heuristic achieves results comparable to the BILP, with 188x less peak memory and 115x faster runtime; trade-offs include 3.9 pp lower demand fulfillment (86.1% vs. 90.0%), larger donor-session distance, higher adverse-reaction donor exposure, and greater invitation burden per non-high-frequency donor, reflecting local versus global optimization. Experiments assess how constraint-aware scheduling can close gaps by mobilizing eligible inactive/lapsing donors.

zh.title: 优化献血员外展以进行献血会议：可扩展决策支持框架
zh.summary: 本文解决了跨多站点网络的献血员招募优化问题，同时平衡资格、需求目标和献血员便利以防止疲劳。该框架使用二进制整数线性规划和贪心启发式算法来实现有效的供需匹配规模。

fr.title: Optimisation des campagnes de sensibilisation des donneurs pour les sessions de collecte de sang : un cadre d'aide à la décision évolutif
fr.summary: Cet article résout l'optimisation du recrutement de donneurs de sang dans les réseaux multi-sites en équilibrant l'admissibilité, les objectifs de demande et la commodité des donneurs pour prévenir la fatigue. Le cadre utilise la programmation linéaire en nombres entiers binaires et les heuristiques gloutonnes pour réaliser une correspondance efficace de l'offre et de la demande à grande échelle.

de.title: Optimierung der Spendengewinnung für Blutspendekampagnen: Ein skalierbares Entscheidungsunterstützungssystem
de.summary: Diese Arbeit befasst sich mit der Optimierung der Blutspenderrekrutierung in Mehrstandort-Netzwerken unter Ausgleich von Berechtigung, Nachfragezielen und Spenderbequemlichkeit, um Müdigkeit zu vermeiden. Das Framework verwendet binäre ganzzahlige lineare Programmierung und Greedy-Heuristiken, um eine effektive Angebot-Nachfrage-Abstimmung im großen Maßstab zu erreichen.

es.title: Optimización del alcance de donantes para sesiones de recolección de sangre: un marco escalable de apoyo a las decisiones
es.summary: Este artículo aborda la optimización del reclutamiento de donantes de sangre en redes multisitio, equilibrando la elegibilidad, los objetivos de demanda y la comodidad del donante para prevenir la fatiga. El marco utiliza programación lineal entera binaria y heurísticas voraces para lograr una coincidencia efectiva de oferta y demanda a escala.

title: View-oriented Conversation Compiler for Agent Trace Analysis
summary: VCC (View-oriented Conversation Compiler) transforms raw agent conversation logs into structured analytical views, improving analysis quality and reducing token consumption. The compiler handles complex nested structures including tool calls, reasoning blocks, and system directives in modern agentic systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.29678v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Agent traces carry increasing analytical value in the era of context learning and harness-driven agentic cognition, yet most prior work treats conversation format as a trivial engineering detail. Modern agent conversations contain deeply structured content, including nested tool calls and results, chain-of-thought reasoning blocks, sub-agent invocations, context-window compaction boundaries, and harness-injected system directives, whose complexity far exceeds that of simple user-assistant exchanges. Feeding such traces to a reflector or other analytical mechanism in plain text, JSON, YAML, or via grep can materially degrade analysis quality. This paper presents VCC (View-oriented Conversation Compiler), a compiler (lex, parse, IR, lower, emit) that transforms raw agent JSONL logs into a family of structured views: a full view (lossless transcript serving as the canonical line-number coordinate system), a user-interface view (reconstructing the interaction as the user actually perceived it), and an adaptive view (a structure-preserving projection governed by a relevance predicate). In a context-learning experiment on AppWorld, replacing only the reflector's input format, from raw JSONL to VCC-compiled views, leads to higher pass rates across all three model configurations tested, while cutting reflector token consumption by half to two-thirds and producing more concise learned memory. These results suggest that message format functions as infrastructure for context learning, not as an incidental implementation choice.

zh.title: 面向视图的会话编译器用于代理追踪分析
zh.summary: VCC（面向视图的会话编译器）将原始代理会话日志转换为结构化分析视图，改进分析质量并减少token消耗。编译器处理现代代理系统中的复杂嵌套结构，包括工具调用、推理块和系统指令。

fr.title: Compilateur de Conversation Orienté Vue pour l'Analyse de Traçage d'Agent
fr.summary: VCC (Compilateur de Conversation Orienté Vue) transforme les journaux bruts de conversation d'agent en vues analytiques structurées, améliorant la qualité d'analyse et réduisant la consommation de tokens. Le compilateur gère les structures imbriquées complexes incluant les appels d'outils, les blocs de raisonnement et les directives système dans les systèmes d'agents modernes.

de.title: Ansichtsorientierter Gesprächscompiler für Agent-Trace-Analyse
de.summary: VCC (Ansichtsorientierter Gesprächscompiler) wandelt rohe Agent-Gesprächsprotokolle in strukturierte analytische Ansichten um, verbessert die Analysqualität und reduziert den Token-Verbrauch. Der Compiler verwaltet komplexe verschachtelte Strukturen einschließlich Werkzeugaufrufe, Überlegungsblöcke und Systemrichtlinien in modernen agentengesteuerten Systemen.

es.title: Compilador de Conversación Orientado a Vista para Análisis de Rastreo de Agente
es.summary: VCC (Compilador de Conversación Orientado a Vista) transforma registros de conversación de agente sin procesar en vistas analíticas estructuradas, mejorando la calidad del análisis y reduciendo el consumo de tokens. El compilador maneja estructuras anidadas complejas, incluidas llamadas de herramientas, bloques de razonamiento y directivas del sistema en sistemas de agentes modernos.

title: Beyond the Steeper Curve: AI-Mediated Metacognitive Decoupling and the Limits of the Dunning-Kruger Metaphor
summary: This paper proposes that LLM use causes 'AI-mediated metacognitive decoupling'—a widening gap between output quality and actual understanding—better explaining overconfidence and over-reliance than the Dunning-Kruger effect alone. The analysis shows AI tools improve task performance while degrading metacognitive accuracy.
content:
arXiv:2603.29681v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The common claim that generative AI simply amplifies the Dunning-Kruger effect is too coarse to capture the available evidence. The clearest findings instead suggest that large language model (LLM) use can improve observable output and short-term task performance while degrading metacognitive accuracy and flattening the classic competence-confidence gradient across skill groups. This paper synthesizes evidence from human-AI interaction, learning research, and model evaluation, and proposes the working model of AI-mediated metacognitive decoupling: a widening gap among produced output, underlying understanding, calibration accuracy, and self-assessed ability. This four-variable account better explains overconfidence, over- and under-reliance, crutch effects, and weak transfer than the simpler metaphor of a uniformly steeper Dunning-Kruger curve. The paper concludes with implications for tool design, assessment, and knowledge work.

zh.title: 超越陡峭曲线：AI介导的元认知解耦与Dunning-Kruger隐喻的局限性
zh.summary: 本文提出LLM的使用导致"AI介导的元认知解耦"——输出质量与实际理解之间的差距扩大——比仅Dunning-Kruger效应更好地解释过度自信和过度依赖。分析表明AI工具改进任务性能，同时降低元认知准确性。

fr.title: Au-delà de la Courbe Plus Raide : Découplage Métacognitif Médié par l'IA et les Limites de la Métaphore de Dunning-Kruger
fr.summary: Cet article propose que l'utilisation des LLM provoque un "découplage métacognitif médié par l'IA"—un écart croissant entre la qualité du résultat et la compréhension réelle—expliquant mieux la surconfiance et la sur-dépendance que l'effet Dunning-Kruger seul. L'analyse montre que les outils d'IA améliorent les performances des tâches tout en dégradant la précision métacognitive.

de.title: Jenseits der steileren Kurve: KI-vermittelte metakognitive Entkopplung und die Grenzen der Dunning-Kruger-Metapher
de.summary: Das Papier schlägt vor, dass die Verwendung von LLMs zu einer "KI-vermittelten metakognitiven Entkopplung" führt—einer sich vergrößernden Kluft zwischen Ausgabequalität und tatsächlichem Verständnis—was Übervertrauen und Überabhängigkeit besser erklärt als nur der Dunning-Kruger-Effekt. Die Analyse zeigt, dass KI-Tools die Aufgabenleistung verbessern, während sie die metakognitive Genauigkeit verschlechtern.

es.title: Más Allá de la Curva Más Pronunciada: Desacoplamiento Metacognitivo Mediado por IA y los Límites de la Metáfora de Dunning-Kruger
es.summary: Este artículo propone que el uso de LLM causa "desacoplamiento metacognitivo mediado por IA"—una brecha creciente entre la calidad del resultado y la comprensión real—explicando mejor la excesiva confianza y la dependencia excesiva que el efecto Dunning-Kruger solo. El análisis muestra que las herramientas de IA mejoran el rendimiento de las tareas mientras degradan la precisión metacognitiva.

title: A First Step Towards Even More Sparse Encodings of Probability Distributions
summary: The paper proposes extracting first-order logical formulas from probability distributions to create sparse encodings requiring significantly fewer values while preserving core information. This approach increases sparsity and generalizability in representing complex probability distributions.
content:
arXiv:2603.29691v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Real world scenarios can be captured with lifted probability distributions. However, distributions are usually encoded in a table or list, requiring an exponential number of values. Hence, we propose a method for extracting first-order formulas from probability distributions that require significantly less values by reducing the number of values in a distribution and then extracting, for each value, a logical formula to be further minimized. This reduction and minimization allows for increasing the sparsity in the encoding while also generalizing a given distribution. Our evaluation shows that sparsity can increase immensely by extracting a small set of short formulas while preserving core information.

zh.title: 迈向更加稀疏的概率分布编码的第一步
zh.summary: 本文提出从概率分布中提取一阶逻辑公式以创建稀疏编码，需要明显更少的值，同时保留核心信息。这种方法增加了表示复杂概率分布的稀疏性和通用性。

fr.title: Un Premier Pas Vers des Encodages Encore Plus Clairsemés de Distributions de Probabilité
fr.summary: L'article propose d'extraire des formules logiques du premier ordre des distributions de probabilité pour créer des encodages clairsemés nécessitant considérablement moins de valeurs tout en préservant les informations essentielles. Cette approche augmente la parcimonie et la généralisabilité dans la représentation des distributions de probabilité complexes.

de.title: Ein erster Schritt zu noch dünneren Kodierungen von Wahrscheinlichkeitsverteilungen
de.summary: Das Papier schlägt vor, Formeln der ersten Ordnung aus Wahrscheinlichkeitsverteilungen zu extrahieren, um spärliche Kodierungen zu erstellen, die erheblich weniger Werte benötigen und gleichzeitig Kerninformationen bewahren. Dieser Ansatz erhöht die Sparsität und Verallgemeinerbarkeit bei der Darstellung komplexer Wahrscheinlichkeitsverteilungen.

es.title: Un Primer Paso Hacia Codificaciones Aún Más Dispersas de Distribuciones de Probabilidad
es.summary: El artículo propone extraer fórmulas lógicas de primer orden de distribuciones de probabilidad para crear codificaciones dispersas que requieren significativamente menos valores mientras se preserva la información central. Este enfoque aumenta la dispersión y la generalizabilidad en la representación de distribuciones de probabilidad complejas.

title: Measuring the metacognition of AI
summary: This methodological paper proposes meta-d' framework and signal detection theory as standards for measuring LLM metacognitive abilities—their capacity to generate reliable confidence ratings and self-regulate decisions based on uncertainty. Experiments on GPT-5, DeepSeek-V3.2-Exp, and Mistral-Medium-2508 demonstrate practical utility.
content:
arXiv:2603.29693v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: A robust decision-making process must take into account uncertainty, especially when the choice involves inherent risks. Because artificial Intelligence (AI) systems are increasingly integrated into decision-making workflows, managing uncertainty relies more and more on the metacognitive capabilities of these systems; i.e, their ability to assess the reliability of and regulate their own decisions. Hence, it is crucial to employ robust methods to measure the metacognitive abilities of AI. This paper is primarily a methodological contribution arguing for the adoption of the meta-d' framework, or its model-free alternatives, as the gold standard for assessing the metacognitive sensitivity of AIs--the ability to generate confidence ratings that distinguish correct from incorrect responses. Moreover, we propose to leverage signal detection theory (SDT) to measure the ability of AIs to spontaneously regulate their decisions based on uncertainty and risk. To demonstrate the practical utility of these psychophysical frameworks, we conduct two series of experiments on three large language models (LLMs)--GPT-5, DeepSeek-V3.2-Exp, and Mistral-Medium-2508. In the first experiments, LLMs performed a primary judgment followed by a confidence rating. In the second, LLMs only performed the primary judgment, while we manipulated the risk associated with either response. On the one hand, applying the meta-d' framework allows us to conduct comparisons along three axes: comparing an LLM to optimality, comparing different LLMs on a given task, and comparing the same LLM across different tasks. On the other hand, SDT allows us to assess whether LLMs become more conservative when risks are high.

zh.title: 测量人工智能的元认知
zh.summary: 这篇方法论论文提议采用meta-d'框架和信号检测理论作为衡量大型语言模型元认知能力的标准——它们生成可靠置信度评分和基于不确定性进行自我调节决策的能力。在GPT-5、DeepSeek-V3.2-Exp和Mistral-Medium-2508上的实验证明了实际效用。

fr.title: Mesurer la métacognition de l'IA
fr.summary: Cet article méthodologique propose le cadre meta-d' et la théorie de la détection du signal comme normes pour mesurer les capacités métacognitives des LLM—leur capacité à générer des évaluations de confiance fiables et à autoréguler les décisions en fonction de l'incertitude. Les expériences sur GPT-5, DeepSeek-V3.2-Exp et Mistral-Medium-2508 démontrent l'utilité pratique.

de.title: Messung der Metakognition von KI
de.summary: Dieses methodische Papier schlägt das meta-d'-Rahmenwerk und die Signaldetektionstheorie als Standards zur Messung der metakognitiven Fähigkeiten von LLMs vor—ihre Fähigkeit, zuverlässige Konfidenzratings zu generieren und Entscheidungen basierend auf Unsicherheit selbst zu regulieren. Experimente mit GPT-5, DeepSeek-V3.2-Exp und Mistral-Medium-2508 zeigen praktischen Nutzen.

es.title: Midiendo la metacognición de la IA
es.summary: Este artículo metodológico propone el marco meta-d' y la teoría de detección de señales como estándares para medir las capacidades metacognitivas de los LLM—su capacidad para generar calificaciones de confianza confiables y autorregular decisiones basadas en la incertidumbre. Los experimentos en GPT-5, DeepSeek-V3.2-Exp y Mistral-Medium-2508 demuestran utilidad práctica.

title: Symphony for Medical Coding: A Next-Generation Agentic System for Scalable and Explainable Medical Coding
summary: Symphony is an agentic system that automates medical coding by reasoning over clinical narratives with direct access to coding guidelines, enabling deployment across any coding system with explainable predictions. Achieving state-of-the-art results across multiple datasets, it addresses the limitations of fixed-code approaches.
content:
arXiv:2603.29709v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Medical coding translates free-text clinical documentation into standardized codes drawn from classification systems that contain tens of thousands of entries and are updated annually. It is central to billing, clinical research, and quality reporting, yet remains largely manual, slow, and error-prone. Existing automated approaches learn to predict a fixed set of codes from labeled data, thereby preventing adaptation to new codes or different coding systems without retraining on different data. They also provide no explanation for their predictions, limiting trust in safety-critical settings. We introduce Symphony for Medical Coding, a system that approaches the task the way expert human coders do: by reasoning over the clinical narrative with direct access to the coding guidelines. This design allows Symphony to operate across any coding system and to provide span-level evidence linking each predicted code to the text that supports it. We evaluate on two public benchmarks and three real-world datasets spanning inpatient, outpatient, emergency, and subspecialty settings across the United States and the United Kingdom. Symphony achieves state-of-the-art results across all settings, establishing itself as a flexible, deployment-ready foundation for automated clinical coding.

zh.title: Symphony医学编码：可扩展且可解释的医学编码下一代代理系统
zh.summary: Symphony是一个代理系统，通过对临床叙述进行推理并直接访问编码指南来自动化医学编码，能够在任何编码系统中部署并提供可解释的预测。在多个数据集上实现最先进的结果，解决了固定代码方法的局限性。

fr.title: Symphony pour le codage médical: un système d'agent nouvelle génération pour le codage médical évolutif et explicable
fr.summary: Symphony est un système d'agent qui automatise le codage médical en raisonnant sur les récits cliniques avec accès direct aux directives de codage, permettant le déploiement sur n'importe quel système de codage avec des prédictions explicables. Réalisant des résultats à la pointe de la technologie sur plusieurs ensembles de données, il aborde les limitations des approches de code fixe.

de.title: Symphony für medizinische Kodierung: ein System der nächsten Generation für skalierbare und erklärbare medizinische Kodierung
de.summary: Symphony ist ein agentenbasiertes System, das die medizinische Kodierung automatisiert, indem es über klinische Narrative mit direktem Zugriff auf Kodierungsrichtlinien verfügt, was eine Bereitstellung über jedes Kodierungssystem mit erklärbaren Vorhersagen ermöglicht. Mit modernsten Ergebnissen über mehrere Datensätze hinweg adressiert es die Grenzen fest kodierter Ansätze.

es.title: Symphony para codificación médica: un sistema agente de próxima generación para codificación médica escalable y explicable
es.summary: Symphony es un sistema agente que automatiza la codificación médica razonando sobre narrativas clínicas con acceso directo a directrices de codificación, permitiendo la implementación en cualquier sistema de codificación con predicciones explicables. Al lograr resultados de última generación en múltiples conjuntos de datos, aborda las limitaciones de los enfoques de código fijo.

title: Reinforced Reasoning for End-to-End Retrosynthetic Planning
summary: ReTriP is an end-to-end generative framework that reformulates retrosynthetic planning in organic chemistry as chain-of-thought reasoning, combining path-coherent molecular representations with curriculum learning and reinforcement learning. The approach achieves state-of-the-art performance with improved robustness in long-horizon planning.
content:
arXiv:2603.29723v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Retrosynthetic planning is a fundamental task in organic chemistry, yet remains challenging due to its combinatorial complexity. To address this, conventional approaches typically rely on hybrid frameworks that combine single-step predictions with external search heuristics, inevitably fracturing the logical coherence between local molecular transformations and global planning objectives. To bridge this gap and embed sophisticated strategic foresight directly into the model's chemical reasoning, we introduce ReTriP, an end-to-end generative framework that reformulates retrosynthesis as a direct Chain-of-Thought reasoning task. We establish a path-coherent molecular representation and employ a progressive training curriculum that transitions from reasoning distillation to reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards, effectively aligning stepwise generation with practical route utility. Empirical evaluation on RetroBench demonstrates that ReTriP achieves state-of-the-art performance, exhibiting superior robustness in long-horizon planning compared to hybrid baselines.

zh.title: 增强推理用于端到端逆合成规划
zh.summary: ReTriP是一个端到端生成框架，将有机化学中的逆合成规划重新表述为思维链推理，结合了路径一致的分子表示与课程学习和强化学习。该方法在长范围规划中实现了最先进的性能和改进的鲁棒性。

fr.title: Raisonnement renforcé pour la planification rétrosynthétique de bout en bout
fr.summary: ReTriP est un framework génératif de bout en bout qui reformule la planification rétrosynthétique en chimie organique comme un raisonnement en chaîne de pensée, combinant des représentations moléculaires cohérentes par chemin avec l'apprentissage curriculaire et l'apprentissage par renforcement. L'approche atteint les performances à la pointe de la technologie avec une robustesse améliorée dans la planification à long horizon.

de.title: Verstärktes Denken für End-to-End-Retrosynthesplanung
de.summary: ReTriP ist ein End-to-End-Generierungsrahmen, der die Retrosynthesplanung in der organischen Chemie als Kettendenken-Reasoning umformuliert und pfadkohärente molekulare Darstellungen mit Curriculum-Learning und Reinforcement Learning kombiniert. Der Ansatz erreicht modernste Leistung mit verbesserter Robustheit in der Langzeitplanung.

es.title: Razonamiento reforzado para planificación retrosintética de extremo a extremo
es.summary: ReTriP es un framework generativo de extremo a extremo que reformula la planificación retrosintética en química orgánica como razonamiento de cadena de pensamiento, combinando representaciones moleculares coherentes con el camino con aprendizaje curricular y aprendizaje por refuerzo. El enfoque logra rendimiento de última generación con robustez mejorada en la planificación de largo horizonte.

title: Spontaneous Functional Differentiation in Large Language Models: A Brain-Like Intelligence Economy
summary: This paper reveals that LLMs spontaneously develop brain-like organizational structures with synergistic cores where information integration exceeds individual parts, emerging as a physical phase transition with increased task difficulty. Ablation studies confirm synergistic components are essential to abstract reasoning capabilities.
content:
arXiv:2603.29735v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The evolution of intelligence in artificial systems provides a unique opportunity to identify universal computational principles. Here we show that large language models spontaneously develop synergistic cores where information integration exceeds individual parts remarkably similar to the human brain. Using Integrated Information Decomposition across multiple architectures we find that middle layers exhibit synergistic processing while early and late layers rely on redundancy. This organization is dynamic and emerges as a physical phase transition as task difficulty increases. Crucially ablating synergistic components causes catastrophic performance loss confirming their role as the physical entity of abstract reasoning and bridging artificial and biological intelligence.

zh.title: 大型语言模型中的自发功能分化：类脑智能经济
zh.summary: 本论文揭示大型语言模型自发发展出类脑组织结构，具有协同核心，其中信息整合超过各个部分，随任务难度增加而产生物理相变。消融研究证实协同成分对抽象推理能力至关重要。

fr.title: Différenciation Fonctionnelle Spontanée dans les Grands Modèles de Langage : Une Économie d'Intelligence Cérébrale
fr.summary: Cet article révèle que les LLM développent spontanément des structures organisationnelles semblables au cerveau avec des noyaux synergiques où l'intégration d'informations dépasse les parties individuelles, émergeant comme une transition de phase physique avec une difficulté accrue des tâches. Les études d'ablation confirment que les composants synergiques sont essentiels aux capacités de raisonnement abstrait.

de.title: Spontane funktionale Differenzierung in großen Sprachmodellen: Eine gehirnähnliche Intelligenzwirtschaft
de.summary: Dieses Papier zeigt, dass LLMs spontan gehirnähnliche Organisationsstrukturen mit synergistischen Kernen entwickeln, bei denen die Informationsintegration die einzelnen Teile übersteigt und mit erhöhter Aufgabenschwierigkeit als physikalischer Phasenübergang auftritt. Ablationsstudien bestätigen, dass synergistische Komponenten für abstrakte Denkfähigkeiten essentiell sind.

es.title: Diferenciación Funcional Espontánea en Grandes Modelos de Lenguaje: Una Economía de Inteligencia Similar al Cerebro
es.summary: Este artículo revela que los LLM desarrollan espontáneamente estructuras organizacionales similares al cerebro con núcleos sinérgicos donde la integración de información supera las partes individuales, emergiendo como una transición de fase física con dificultad de tarea aumentada. Los estudios de ablación confirman que los componentes sinérgicos son esenciales para las capacidades de razonamiento abstracto.

title: CausalPulse: An Industrial-Grade Neurosymbolic Multi-Agent Copilot for Causal Diagnostics in Smart Manufacturing
summary: CausalPulse is a production-ready multi-agent system deployed at Robert Bosch that unifies anomaly detection, causal discovery, and root-cause analysis through neurosymbolic architecture for real-time manufacturing diagnostics. It achieves 98%+ success rates with 50-60s end-to-end latency per diagnostic workflow.
content:
arXiv:2603.29755v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Modern manufacturing environments demand real-time, trustworthy, and interpretable root-cause insights to sustain productivity and quality. Traditional analytics pipelines often treat anomaly detection, causal inference, and root-cause analysis as isolated stages, limiting scalability and explainability. In this work, we present CausalPulse, an industry-grade multi-agent copilot that automates causal diagnostics in smart manufacturing. It unifies anomaly detection, causal discovery, and reasoning through a neurosymbolic architecture built on standardized agentic protocols. CausalPulse is being deployed in a Robert Bosch manufacturing plant, integrating seamlessly with existing monitoring workflows and supporting real-time operation at production scale. Evaluations on both public (Future Factories) and proprietary (Planar Sensor Element) datasets show high reliability, achieving overall success rates of 98.0% and 98.73%. Per-criterion success rates reached 98.75% for planning and tool use, 97.3% for self-reflection, and 99.2% for collaboration. Runtime experiments report end-to-end latency of 50-60s per diagnostic workflow with near-linear scalability (R^2=0.97), confirming real-time readiness. Comparison with existing industrial copilots highlights distinct advantages in modularity, extensibility, and deployment maturity. These results demonstrate how CausalPulse's modular, human-in-the-loop design enables reliable, interpretable, and production-ready automation for next-generation manufacturing.

zh.title: CausalPulse：用于智能制造因果诊断的工业级神经符号多代理副驾驶
zh.summary: CausalPulse是一个生产级多代理系统，部署在罗伯特·博世公司，通过神经符号架构统一异常检测、因果发现和根本原因分析，用于实时制造诊断。每个诊断工作流实现98%以上的成功率，端到端延迟为50-60秒。

fr.title: CausalPulse : Un Copilote Multi-Agent Neurosymbolique de Qualité Industrielle pour le Diagnostic Causal dans la Fabrication Intelligente
fr.summary: CausalPulse est un système multi-agent prêt pour la production déployé chez Robert Bosch qui unifie la détection d'anomalies, la découverte causale et l'analyse des causes profondes par une architecture neurosymbolique pour le diagnostic de fabrication en temps réel. Il réalise des taux de succès de 98%+ avec une latence bout en bout de 50-60s par flux de travail diagnostique.

de.title: CausalPulse: Ein industrietauglicher neurosymbolischer Multi-Agent-Kopilot zur Kausaldiagnose in der intelligenten Fertigung
de.summary: CausalPulse ist ein produktionsreifer Multi-Agent-System, das bei Robert Bosch eingesetzt ist und Anomalieerkennung, kausale Entdeckung und Grundursachenanalyse durch eine neurosymbolische Architektur für Echtzeit-Fertigungsdiagnose vereint. Es erreicht Erfolgsquoten von 98%+ mit 50-60s End-to-End-Latenz pro diagnostischem Arbeitsablauf.

es.title: CausalPulse: Un Copiloto Multi-Agente Neurosimbólico de Grado Industrial para Diagnóstico Causal en Fabricación Inteligente
es.summary: CausalPulse es un sistema multi-agente listo para producción implementado en Robert Bosch que unifica detección de anomalías, descubrimiento causal y análisis de causa raíz a través de arquitectura neurosimbólica para diagnóstico de fabricación en tiempo real. Logra tasas de éxito del 98%+ con latencia de extremo a extremo de 50-60s por flujo de trabajo de diagnóstico.

title: Tracking vs. Deciding: The Dual-Capability Bottleneck in Searchless Chess Transformers
summary: This paper identifies a fundamental bottleneck in chess-playing transformers: models must learn state tracking and decision-making simultaneously, but these require contradictory data distributions, limiting overall performance. Using model scaling and Elo-weighted training, they achieved Lichess bullet rating 2570 without search.
content:
arXiv:2603.29761v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: A human-like chess engine should mimic the style, errors, and consistency of a strong human player rather than maximize playing strength. We show that training from move sequences alone forces a model to learn two capabilities: state tracking, which reconstructs the board from move history, and decision quality, which selects good moves from that reconstructed state. These impose contradictory data requirements: low-rated games provide the diversity needed for tracking, while high-rated games provide the quality signal for decision learning. Removing low-rated data degrades performance.
  We formalize this tension as a dual-capability bottleneck, P <= min(T,Q), where overall performance is limited by the weaker capability. Guided by this view, we scale the model from 28M to 120M parameters to improve tracking, then introduce Elo-weighted training to improve decisions while preserving diversity. A 2 x 2 factorial ablation shows that scaling improves tracking, weighting improves decisions, and their combination is superadditive. Linear weighting works best, while overly aggressive weighting harms tracking despite lower validation loss. We also introduce a coverage-decay formula, t* = log(N/kcrit)/log b, as a reliability horizon for intra-game degeneration risk.
  Our final 120M-parameter model, without search, reached Lichess bullet 2570 over 253 rated games. On human move prediction it achieves 55.2% Top-1 accuracy, exceeding Maia-2 rapid and Maia-2 blitz. Unlike position-based methods, sequence input naturally encodes full game history, enabling history-dependent decisions that single-position models cannot exhibit.

zh.title: 跟踪 vs. 决策：无搜索国际象棋变换器中的双重能力瓶颈
zh.summary: 本论文确定了国际象棋变换器中的基本瓶颈：模型必须同时学习状态跟踪和决策制定，但这些需要相互矛盾的数据分布，限制了整体性能。使用模型扩展和Elo加权训练，他们在没有搜索的情况下实现了Lichess子弹等级2570。

fr.title: Suivi vs. Décision : Le Goulot d'Étranglement de Capacité Duelle dans les Transformers d'Échecs sans Recherche
fr.summary: Cet article identifie un goulot d'étranglement fondamental dans les transformers jouant aux échecs : les modèles doivent apprendre le suivi d'état et la prise de décision simultanément, mais ces derniers nécessitent des distributions de données contradictoires, limitant les performances globales. En utilisant la mise à l'échelle des modèles et l'entraînement pondéré par Elo, ils ont atteint une note Lichess bullet 2570 sans recherche.

de.title: Verfolgung vs. Entscheidung: Der Dual-Capability-Engpass in suchfreien Schach-Transformern
de.summary: Dieses Papier identifiziert einen fundamentalen Engpass in Schach-spielenden Transformern: Modelle müssen gleichzeitig Zustandsverfolgung und Entscheidungsfindung lernen, aber diese erfordern widersprüchliche Datenverteilungen, die die Gesamtleistung begrenzen. Unter Verwendung von Modellskalierung und Elo-gewichtetes Training erreichten sie eine Lichess Bullet-Bewertung von 2570 ohne Suche.

es.title: Rastreo vs. Toma de Decisiones: El Cuello de Botella de Capacidad Dual en Transformers de Ajedrez sin Búsqueda
es.summary: Este artículo identifica un cuello de botella fundamental en transformers que juegan ajedrez: los modelos deben aprender el rastreo de estado y la toma de decisiones simultáneamente, pero estos requieren distribuciones de datos contradictorias, limitando el desempeño general. Utilizando escalado de modelos y entrenamiento ponderado por Elo, lograron una calificación Lichess bullet 2570 sin búsqueda.
